entry.title: Useful links to align SAP SuccessFactors with the EU Pay Transparency Directive
entry.content:
Introduction

 

This blog provides links to the EU Pay Transparency Directive (2026) and how to align with it in SAP SuccessFactors. 

 

Links

 

  • A great blog by Phil MacGovern from Effective People that summarizes the different areas/modules that can be impacted as well as links to the official EU website describing the changes.

 

  • All the out of the box Stories available through Content Store.

 

  • Step by step demo recordings by @CSmith on how to build Compensation module stories:
    • Learn How to Create A Compensation Budget Dashboard in Report Stories
    • Learn How to Create an Incentive Outcome Dashboard in Report Stories

 

 

 

If you hear of specific features in one of the next SAP SuccessFactors releases that are specifically addressing this Directive, please let us know in the comments !

 

 

All the best,

Xavier

 

(if you found this blog useful please consider giving it a like)

entry.title: How to deactivate notification for SAP GUI Scripting?
entry.content:
Hi,

I want to know how to disable notification for SAP GUI, specifically via registry key.


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: How to deactivate notification for SAP GUI Scripting?
entry.content:
Hello,

It is possible to disable via the SAP GUI options, on the "Scripting" section.

And the correspondent registry values are:

For the "Notify when a script attaches to SAP GUI" option: 

[HKEY_CURRENT_USER\Software\SAP\SAPGUI Front\SAP Frontend Server\Security] WarnOnAttach (REG_DWORD) [Default: 1] {0 = inactive; 1 = active}

For the "Notify when a script opens a connection" option:

[HKEY_CURRENT_USER\Software\SAP\SAPGUI Front\SAP Frontend Server\Security] WarnOnConnection (REG_DWORD) [Default: 1] {0 = inactive; 1 = active}

entry.title: Integrating with Snowflake Using SAP Cloud Integration: A Deep Dive into the Snowflake Adapter
entry.content:
In today’s data-driven business world, cloud data platforms have become the backbone of scalable and real-time analytics. One such powerhouse is Snowflake — a cloud-native data warehouse designed for performance, concurrency, and simplicity.

What is Snowflake in a Nutshell?

Snowflake is a cloud-based data platform that allows organizations to store and analyze vast amounts of structured and semi-structured data. 

Snowflake Adapter in SAP CI

As part of SAP’s Integration Suite, SAP Cloud Integration (CI) offers connectivity to various third-party systems, and with the Snowflake adapter, it becomes possible to directly interact with your Snowflake data warehouse — automating business processes that rely on real-time or batch data movement.

Snowflake Adapter is your one-stop data management tool to interact and execute operations on Snowflake.

  • Bulk Export and Import: Facilitates importing from a range of input formats like CSV, JSON, Avro, etc. Similarly, you can export data to your desired format using the unload capability.
  • Snowflake Operations Support: Offers support for all Snowflake operations along with SQL support for advanced users.
  • Ease of Access and Connectivity: Enables saving of connection-related details on our platform to allow simplified operations and querying.
  • Multiple options for Staging Storage: Various options to load data into staging storage like Amazon S3, Google Cloud Storage, or Microsoft Azure.
  • Dynamic configuration with headers and properties: Assigning dynamic values to different properties allows enhanced flexibility to your integration flows. You can also refer to dynamic parameters using SAP Cloud Integration exchange headers and properties.

In this blog post, We will take a deep dive into the following operations mentioned below:

  • Insert – Load new data into Snowflake tables.

  • Select – Query data for reporting or downstream integration.

  • Delete – Remove specific records as part of data cleansing or archiving.

  • Execute – Run SQL statements

  • Tablelist – Retrieve metadata and structure information of Snowflake tables.

  • Update – Modify existing records based on dynamic business rules.

 

Snowflake:

Lets get started with the configurations that are needed to create Database and the Table on Snowflake:

1. Log on to your Snowflake account and in left panel select Data --> Databases.

Please note: You need to System Admin role to create Tables in Snowflake

2. In the Database section, click on + Database button on the top right and create a database with a desired name, I created a Database with name "TESTING"

3. Once the Database is created, you will see two schemas "INFORMATION_SCHEMA" and "PUBLIC", select any schema under which you intend to create the Table. I selected "PUBLIC" schema to create the Table

I am using Standard option to create the Table.

4. At this step you need to define the schema of your table and click on "Create Table"

The Table name that I created is "ACCOUNT", and it looks below :

We are good on Snowflake side, Lets dive into our SAP CI Tenant and check on the Snowflake Adapter.

SAP Cloud Integration Tenant:

In this blog, I will performing operations like Insert, Delete, Update, Table List, Execute and Select. I have created a simple iflow where I will be sending the data from Postman and perform the Operations on Snowflake using Snowflake Receiver Adapter.

Snowflake Receiver Adapter:

The connection tab contains the following fields:

Field

Description

Authentication

Select the Database Account method as the authentication mechanism.

Credential Name Alias

Specify the artifact Name that stores the username/password credentials in the Security Material.

Address

Specify the JDBC endpoint URL of the Snowflake application to be used for the connection. This address typically contains an account identifier which is a combination of the organization and account name separated by a hyphen(orgname-account_name).

Database

Specify the name of the database. "TESTING" in our case

Schema

Specify the name of the schema to be accessed. "Public" in our case

Warehouse

Specify the name of the Snowflake warehouse. If the warehouse provided is not found/incorrect, it uses DEFAULT.

1. Operation - Insert

Insert Operation is used to insert a record into the Table, Lets populated the Table "ACCOUNT" with some records.

Request Payload: It is mandatory to include metadata of the structure with datatype attribute for fieldname in the INSERT payload. The metadata/row tags are case sensitive and must be in lower case.

<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<ROOT>
	<metadata>
		<fieldname datatype="VARCHAR">ID</fieldname>
		<fieldname datatype="VARCHAR">LASTNAME</fieldname>
		<fieldname datatype="VARCHAR">FIRSTNAME</fieldname>
		<fieldname datatype="VARCHAR">SALUTATION</fieldname>
		<fieldname datatype="VARCHAR">FULLNAME</fieldname>
	</metadata>
	<row>
		<ID>00QJ1000004yZSiMAM</ID>
		<LASTNAME>Oswal</LASTNAME>
		<FIRSTNAME>Punith</FIRSTNAME>
		<SALUTATION>Mr.</SALUTATION>
		<FULLNAME>Punith Oswal</FULLNAME>
	</row>
</ROOT>

Request sent from Postman:

Message Successful in SAP CI:

Record inserted in Snowflake:

2. Operation - Update :

Used to Update an existing record in the Table.

Lets update the existing record that we created in the Insert Operation and Change Firstname from Punith to Rohit and Fullname to Rohit Oswal

Payload for Update: "where" field is mandatory in the payload, it acts as a where clause for the record to get updated.

<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<ROOT>
	<metadata>
		<fieldname datatype="VARCHAR">ID</fieldname>
		<fieldname datatype="VARCHAR">LASTNAME</fieldname>
		<fieldname datatype="VARCHAR">FIRSTNAME</fieldname>
		<fieldname datatype="VARCHAR">SALUTATION</fieldname>
		<fieldname datatype="VARCHAR">FULLNAME</fieldname>
	</metadata>
	<row>
		<ID>00QJ1000004yZSiMAM</ID>
		<LASTNAME>Oswal</LASTNAME>
		<FIRSTNAME>Rohit</FIRSTNAME>
		<SALUTATION>Mr.</SALUTATION>
		<FULLNAME>Rohit Oswal</FULLNAME>
		<where>ID='00QJ1000004yZSiMAM'</where>
	</row>
</ROOT>

Request sent from Postman:

Message successful in SAP CI

Record Updated in Snowflake:

3. Operation - Delete :

Used to Delete an existing record in the Table.

Currently there are 2 records in the "Account Table", lets delete record with Firstname "Rohit".

There is no request payload needed for DELETE operation, Just trigger a blank request from the Postman.

Message successful in SAP CI:

 

 

 

 

Record with FIRSTNAME = Rohit deleted in Snowflake Table:

4. Operation - Table List :

This operation lists the tables for the current specified schema.

This operation lists the all details of the Tables present under the schema, Schema = PUBLIC in our case.

There is no request payload needed for Table List operation, Just trigger a blank request from the Postman.

Message successful in SAP CI:

Response from Snowflake:

5. Operation - SELECT : 

Select is used to fetch data from the table. You can customize data by selecting required columns in Response Fields for a specific condition using Where Clause. You can use Limit to fetch a
limited number of records from a predefined Offset value and finally, Orderby Statement allows
you to return records in descending or ascending order.

Currently there are 2 records in the "Account Table", lets fetch the Record ID = 

12QJ1000004yZSiPPP with response fields - ID,FULLNAME

There is no request payload needed for SELECT operation, Just trigger a blank request from the Postman.

Message Successful in SAP CI:

Response from Snowflake:

6. Operation - EXECUTE : 

Execute allows you to run SQL queries or stored procedures on the target table.

Lets delete our entire table using EXECUTE operation, using SQL statement - DROP TABLE ACCOUNT

There is no request payload needed for EXECUTE operation, Just trigger a blank request from the Postman.

Message successful in SAP CI:

Response from Snowflake:

Table "ACCOUNT" deleted in Snowflake account.

Conclusion:

In this blog, we explored key Snowflake adapter operations in SAP CI — from Insert and Update to Select and Execute — laying the groundwork for seamless data integration. These core actions empower you to connect SAP with Snowflake efficiently for real-time and batch scenarios.

In the next blog, I’ll cover advanced operations like Bulk Upsert and UNLOAD — stay tuned!

 

Hope this helps!

Cheers,

Punith Oswal

entry.title: Issue applying Hierarchy on a non key column in Dimension table in SAP Datasphere
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community,

I am currently working in SAP Datasphere and need assistance regarding applying a hierarchy on a non-key attribute in a dimension table.

Objective:
I would like to apply a hierarchy on the ECONOMIC ACTIVITY attribute (which contains NACE codes) in the PARTY dimension table.

My Setup:

  • I have a local table named PARTY, where:

    • PARTY_ID is the primary key

    • ECONOMIC ACTIVITY (technical name: ECNMC_ACTVTY) is a regular attribute (not a key)

  • I have created a separate NACE Code Hierarchy table, with semantic usage set as "Dimension with Hierarchy Directory"

    • The hierarchy is defined using standard parent-child relationships

  • I also created a hierarchy directory dimension to represent the NACE code levels, and associated it with the ECONOMIC ACTIVITY field in the PARTY table via a text/association mapping

In the Analytic Model:

  • The Analytic Model is built on top of a FACT table

  • The FACT table is joined to the PARTY table using RELEVANT BUSINESS PARTNER ID → PARTY_ID

  • The join is working as expected, and the RELEVANT BUSINESS PARTNER ID node exposes dimensions from the PARTY table, including ECONOMIC ACTIVITY

  • However, the hierarchy is only available or shown on RELEVANT BUSINESS PARTNER ID, and not on ECONOMIC ACTIVITY

Question:
Why is the hierarchy not appearing for ECONOMIC ACTIVITY in the Analytic Model? Am I missing a step in exposing or enabling the hierarchy on a non-key attribute? Or is it the case that hierarchies in SAP Datasphere are only supported for primary key fields in dimension tables?

Any clarification or guidance would be greatly appreciated.

 

 

Thanks,

Mayura

entry.title: Rates 2.0: Exploring the Latest Enhancements in SAP Fieldglass
entry.content:
Have you heard Rates 2.0 has been released, and that it’s made faster and simpler than ever to create and work with rates? No? 

Allow me to tell you: Rates 2.0 is here, trending, and with exciting updates in the latest release. The enhancements were designed to streamline processes and improve efficiency for users managing statements of work (SOW) and service contracts.

Let's dive into the four key updates: 

  • SOW Worker Role Custom Lookup: The new feature lets you see the value [ALL] for the SOW Worker Role Site Code value key. This means you can now associate site codes in bulk that are valid for a specific SOW worker role and supplier combination, simplifying the management of site structures.
  • External Contract ID as Key for Rates Lookup: For those using the Contracts feature for Services, the 'External Contract ID' field is now available as a lookup key in the Rate and Rate Component custom lookups. This enhancement provides a more efficient way to manage service contracts by leveraging existing contract identifiers.
  • Improved SOW Worker Rate Management: The Effective Date feature now integrates with the Rates 2.0 rate structure, allowing users to determine all relevant rate values for the entire SOW period using the rate Valid On dates. This improvement ensures accurate rate management throughout the duration of a project.
  • Configuration Validation Disabled for Rates 2.0 Companies: Rates 2.0 can now be enabled for new or existing companies without the need for ‘Use Rate Grid for SOW Workers’ company configuration enablement. This change simplifies the setup process, making it easier for companies to adopt the latest rate management features. 

 

For more insights, we invite you to check our document Rates 2.0 Step-by-Step Guide (SAP Customer). It's packed with valuable information like how to create rates from scratch in the new format, what is needed for coexistence with Rates 1.0, and more.

entry.title: [MDK] CreateRelatedMedia override media property from rule not working
entry.content:
I am using MDK version 24.11.3-001 on Android.  I have an Attachment Control where the user will be adding several images at once, then will click a Save button to upload the attachments to the backend.  The backend is a v2 OData service, and I have a Media entity set that is access through a navigation path (eg /Delivery('123')/Attachment/$value).

I have a CreateRelatedMedia action to perform the upload.  Once the save is complete, I would like to take another action, such as closing the page and returning to the list.  Furthermore, I'd like to add a SLUG header to include the filename for each individual file in the attachment control.

To accomplish these two desires, I believe the best way would be to loop through the attachments in a rule, and execute the action by overriding the properties for each loop.  The documentation indicates that the Media property should be an object with a "content" property and a "contentType" property.  

    let attachments = clientAPI.evaluateTargetPath('#Page:DeliveryAttachment/#Control:FormCellAttachment0/#Value');
    let uploadPromises = [];
    for (let i = 0; i < attachments.length; i++) {
        const attachment = attachments[i];
        const content = attachment.content;
        const contentType = attachment.contentType;
        const filename = attachment.urlString;
        const actionPromise = clientAPI.executeAction({
            "Name": '/myapp/Actions/DeliveryAttachments/OData/CreateDeliveryAttachmentRelatedMedia.action',
            "Properties": {
                "Media": {
                    "content": content,
                    "contentType": contentType
                },
                "Headers": {
                    "SLUG": filename
                }         
            }
        });
        uploadPromises.push(actionPromise);        
    }
    return Promise.all(uploadPromises).then(
        () => {
            clientAPI.executeAction('/myapp/Actions/ClosePage.action')
        }
    );

 I've tested this on both Web and Android and I get an exception from the MDK code (although each version throws an exception at different spots - I'm really only concerned with the Android functionality, so below I've shown the error from the Android device).  I've tried several different variations of this code, so I think the code is correct, but please let me know if I've done something wrong in my code.

On the Android device, the exception is raised from node_modules/mdk-sap/ODate/crud/ODataRelatedMediaCreator.ts:

As mentioned I get a similar error in Web, although from a different place in the code.

Have I encountered an issue or is there something I'm doing wrong in the code, or should I take a completely different approach?  (Note I've tried just executing the action and using the attachment control #Value in the media property, which will upload each attachment in the control - but the issue is that if I put another action in the Success Action, this success action gets executed for each attachment in the control).

Thanks!

entry.title: My Sapphire Orlando 2025 recap from an SAP Community point of view
entry.content:
Hi everybody!  Mr. bluebeard here and I wanted to give you a recap of my experience at this year's SAP Sapphire 2025 event in Orlando, Florida. 

The Show Floor

When I got to Orlando, it was a lot like coming home because I've been to several Sapphires but there's nothing like that feeling when you approach the Orange County Convention Center and you know that you're about to go through an amazing entranceway and see a beautiful show floor.  Well, that's what happened.  The SAP team designed an amazing show entrance that was very photo worthy.  The show floor had lots of vendor and partner booths to the left, and a keynote stage auditorium to the right as well as a broadcast studio for virtual attendee presentations.  Along the sides we several large and small theaters for dedicated deep dive presentations, and a meeting center in the back.  There were several areas to relax and take phone calls as well, but most amazing to me was the center hallway of the show floor.

entrance before escalator down show floor main walkway

The center hallway started off with the SAP Experience Center, which I'm part of in Team SIX (Solution, Industries and Experience), the SIX team is responsible for designing and giving people a great experience going through all the innovations that SAP software is enabling. For this Experience Center you queued up outside like you were waiting at an amusement park and then you enter and you are informed about the persona journey that you're about to partake in, much like you would at an SAP Experience Center at an SAP office.  You pick a role like CEO, CPO, CHRO, then you get a AirTag that syncs to an Android or an iPhone so you can browse all the learnings on your mobile if you didn't have time to complete everything in the Experience Center.  They brought in a industrial injection machine, a warehouse robot set up to show how you could repair that equipment if it broke using AI. Once you get done in the experience center and you exit the enclosed area you're in the center hallway again.  You see a lot of beautiful booths with experts from BTP, Applications and a lot of people that are just having conversations with our customers and partners about SAP technology.

experience center experience center guided tourairtag delivery robot arm

Then when you reach the back edge of the hallway before you get to the meeting event center that's where you see the great Learning and Certification Lab. We had a 25 person auditorium theater built into it, several different touch panels where you could choose to take a picture of yourself and have AI generate a graphic image of you tied to your user name. After completing quizzes your image & name were listed on a leaderboard for all to see.  But also as you tapped your badge at the monitors and completed knowledge exercises you accumulated learning power and the tree of learning would glow brighter the more you learned. 

Andre Bechtold preso in Lab leaderboardLearning and Certification Lab Sitting area outside labtree of learning

Now onto what I did while in the space.  In the theater at the back of the Learning and Certification Lab is where myself and one of our SAP mentors @DanielleLarocca, as well as @anita_riegel from team SIX all got together and presented in a panel format "How to Harness the Power of the SAP Community".  It was a 25 minute chat to explain to people what the SAP community is and how they can benefit from it because it's their community.  Everyone who was sitting in that room was a member of the community just by being there.  Maybe they don't use the website, so we directed them to community.sap.com and how they can use those resources once signed up, but also being present at Sapphire, SAP TechEd, SAP Inside Tracks, Meetups and attending things like an an SAP CodeJam allow for community interaction in person, around the world.

 

Also I just met and talked with people outside of the SAP learning certification lab when I wasn't in a meeting.  Speaking of meetings, a lot of my time was spent working with people that are part of the SAP Mentors and Champions programs, enjoying conversations with them about what we can do to make our community experiences better.  They met with SAP executives and leaders within several SAP organizations and expressed direct feedback, so it was very nice to be a part of those meetings along with @StephanieMarley 

Then I went to a meeting with the SAP AppHaus team, and learned more about their network of SAP AppHaus locations and what they're doing with the partner network to expand the reach of the AppHaus methodology and how we can host things like a CodeJam, or do other customer events inside of these AppHaus locations which are optimized to take people through the SAP AppHaus methodology for solving their business problems.

AppHaus meeting

My Favorite Moment

I met a lot of new friends as well at after hours events like the AWS Networking Session. One was a gentleman from Mexico, Adrian, who works for a lighting industry company.   It was honestly my favorite moment because I got to hear his story about what it was like to attend Sapphire in Philadelphia (where i live) in 1996 as well as SAP TechEd that year, so really cool stories comparing not only those early events but the changes to the software we are presenting now that his company utilizes.  Adrian has been around for a long time and he has seen the change of SAP software as well as the differences between on premise and cloud, and now the integration of AI and how businesses utilize that software. 

The Keynotes

The keynotes were great from a high level. I attended all the keynote sessions at the keynote theater, which is a large space on the show floor that was full, even into the stadium seating outside.  Seeing all the great presentations with the things that we're doing from all the executives was awesome, but seeing a friend of mine, Abishek, from the old SAP mobile team who is now moved through his career up to the point where he's working for NBC Universal and was one of the customers on stage presenting how they are using all these great things was so fun and rewarding to see. 

A few of the announcements from the keynotes:

  • Intelligent Apps for the SAP Business Data Cloud
  • Joule AI assistant availability
  • Perplexity partnership
  • AI Foundation
  • many more.... 

keynote selfie Thomas Saueressig at keynoteoverflow seating

 

Celebration Night

Then we had a great event at Disney's Epcot Center Park where SAP entertained everyone who came to Sapphire by renting out the entire park.  We had two opportunities to listen to some music from the concert artist, The Zac Brown Band, and just if you wanted to ride rides or enter into any of the different country themed sections of Epcot Park you could do that.  There were several places to grab food, snacks, drinks, and just talk to all your friends and colleagues from SAP.   It was a nice night, but a little humid as Florida tends to be. I made it halfway around the world...

Epcot entrance Zac Brown

Epcot at night

   

In Summary

Finally flying home after a long week of meetings and having great interactions with all the people on the show floor reminds me it is such an honor to get to do what I do.  As I like to say, I have a "get to job".  I get to do this!  And I enjoyed reflecting by looking at the tons of pictures that I took, especially for the Selfies with bluebeard post, Well over 30 people commented on my beard which was so great cause I used that as an ice breaker to get a pic.  I know @moshenaveh has asked other people attending Sapphire to write up their experiences from this year's Sapphire and I look forward to reading those recaps. 

It was a certifiably good time.  Hope to see you at the next event.

who, what, where?!?

entry.title: SAP Developer News May 29th, 2025
entry.content:
DESCRIPTION

PODCAST:  https://podcast.opensap.info/sap-developers/2025/05/29/sap-developer-news-may-29th-2025/

SAP BTP ABAP Environment – Release 2505

  • Blog post: https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blog-posts-by-sap/sap-btp-abap-environment-release-2505/ba-p/14102638
  • What's New in ABAP Generative AI in ABAP Cloud: https://help.sap.com/whats-new/4d359a7f8e8248e18f98e85ea0b9d83e?locale=en-US

Studio Home in SAP Build

  • Introducing Studio Home, new decision management application for business users

https://community.sap.com/t5/application-development-and-automation-blog-posts/empowering-business-to-manage-decisions-in-sap-build-process-automation/ba-p/14092130

  • Configure Studio Home application in Work zone – SAP Help Portal

https://help.sap.com/docs/build-process-automation/sap-build-process-automation/configure-studio-home-application

SAP CAP Developer Challenge June

  • Blog post: https://community.sap.com/t5/application-development-and-automation-blog-posts/sap-community-escape-house-for-june-sap-cap-%EF%B8%8F-%EF%B8%8F/ba-p/14110094
  • SAP Community Escape House: https://sap-community-escape-house.cfapps.us10.hana.ondemand.com/

SAPUI5 WCAG 2.2 Update

  • WCAG Homepage: https://www.w3.org/WAI/
  • WCAG 2.2 Changes: https://www.w3.org/WAI/standards-guidelines/wcag/new-in-22/
  • SAPUI5 is Leading the Way in Accessibility with WCAG 2.2 Blog Post: https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blog-posts-by-sap/sapui5-is-leading-the-way-in-accessibility-with-wcag-2-2/ba-p/14101596

 New learning content for SAP Integration Suite

  • SAP Integration Suite Basic Trial: https://community.sap.com/t5/integration-blog-posts/sap-integration-suite-basic-trial/ba-p/14112484
  • Start your free trial for SAP Integration Suite: https://www.sap.com/products/technology-platform/integration-suite/trial.html
  • Get Started with SAP Integration Suite, advanced event mesh: https://developers.sap.com/mission.advanced-event-mesh-get-started.html

 

CHAPTER TITLES

0:00 Intro

0:10 SAP BTP ABAP Environment – Release 2505

1:29 Studio Home in SAP Build

3:38 SAP CAP Developer Challenge June

4:37 SAPUI5 WCAG 2.2 Update

5:18 New learning content for SAP Integration Suite

 

TRANSCRIPTION

[Intro] This is the SAP Developer News for May the 29th, 2025.

[Shilpa] Hello, ABAP Developers. The ABAP release of 2505 happened last week, and I'm here to highlight some of the key features. Firstly, reduction in the technical downtime during the release upgrade, which is great to keep your business process running. Secondly, in ADT, there is a new wizard called Quick Fiori Application Generator, which will help you to generate a Fiori project within ADT itself. You need not have to log into Business Application Studio or Visual Studio Code to create a Fiori project, but it is integrated with ADT. A video about this feature will be available coming Monday in our SAP Developers YouTube channel. And thirdly and most important one, AI in ABAP. In the AI space, I want to highlight three things. First, Joule Chat is now integrated in ABAP repository object generator wizard, which will help you to generate OData UI service from the scratch just by using natural language prompts. Second, Unit test is quite important, and you can now use the prompt to generate ABAP Unit test methods. Third, the prediction of the code completion has been enhanced. There are other features related to ADT, OData, CDS, which is described in the blog post by Nora Klemp, which is in the description. Do check it out.

[Shrinivasan] Decisions in SAP Build allows business users to create business rules for approval policies, pricing logic, visibility check, etc. within a process automation project. Until now, even the simplest rule modifications required developer intervention, leading to delayed updates and a slow change management cycle due to their dependency. That's where Studio Home, SAP Build's latest decision management application comes in. With the new Studio Home, business users can now view, edit and maintain decisions from a dedicated user interface that only shows the decision artifacts within a project. Options such as inputs, outputs, reorder, create new rule are not present in this user interface, showing only the options that are relevant to the business users With a one-click button, they can release a patch version of the project and deploy it after making the rule updates This application comes with a design console that shows any error or warning message during the release and deployment of a project and a version history option to navigate and view the previous versions of the decision artifacts in the project. The Studio Home application can be configured to be accessed from a work zone tile. The instructions to configure the application in the work zone site is provided in the help portal. On top of Studio Home's release, there are enhancements to the expression editor in decision tables, making quick edits faster and more intuitive than ever before. With a new Studio Home, business users can now take the ownership of maintaining and owning the decision artifacts independently. By simplifying the process, reducing dependencies, and accelerating change management, decision management is more accessible and efficient than ever. Check out this blog post from Sesh Sreenivas to find out all the details about Studio Home and the enhancements to the expression editor for decisions.

[Ajay] Hello everyone. We have almost come to the end of this month and we thank all the participants who have already completed the ABAP Developer Challenge. We still have a couple of days to finish the ABAP Developer Challenge and earn a badge of completion. In June, we get to escape a brand new room for SAP Cloud Application Programming Model in SAP Community Escape House. This room will be unlocked next week for the participants to attempt the SAP Developer Challenge. In order to escape the CAP room, you would have to create a CAP project, create some custom code, and perform some testing. On your way out, you will also learn about remote debugging. In order to participate, you just need to use your user ideas and input and start the challenge and explore the story that unfolds. Links to the SAP Community's Cave House are included in the description. Wishing you all the best for your challenge and happy learning!

[Michelle] SAP has announced that its SAPUI5 Web Development Framework now complies with the latest Web Content Accessibility Guidelines, WCAG 2.2 and SAPUI5 version 1.136 and UI5 Web Components version 2.9.0. These updates enhance features like focus visibility and assistive technology, ensuring better usability for people with disabilities and improve the user experience overall. If you're interested to learn more of what kind of changes have been made to the SAP UI5 and UI5 Web Components frameworks, be sure to check out the link in the description below. Happy coding!

[Antonio] Hola, SAP developers. I'm always amazed by how accessible SAP learning content is nowadays. Long gone are the days that the only way you could learn about an SAP technology was by attending a course at a training center. I just want to highlight two new learning offerings that are available to all of you developers out there who are interested in learning about integration. There is the new SAP Integration Suite basic trial, which provides you a ready-made environment where you can get familiar with a few capabilities, like for example, cloud integration, API management, and integration assessment. It should take you around an hour 30 minutes to complete it, so go ahead and check it out. Also, there's a new mission in the tutorial system, which helps you get started with SAP Integration Suite Advanced Event Mesh. It covers everything, from creating an instance to connecting to an S4HANA cloud system, getting familiar with the main components of the service, and how you can integrate with other products, like cloud integration and SAP Build Process Automation. All the content is freely available. It is just a matter of dedicating time to learn. So, go ahead and enjoy the learning. Happy learning!

entry.title: Issue Creating Custom Transactions ZIQ01 and ZIQ02 for Standard Transactions IQ01 and IQ02
entry.content:
Dear SAP Community,

I am attempting to create two custom transactions (ZIQ01 and ZIQ02) that will call the standard SAP transactions IQ01 and IQ02, respectively. Our goal is to have these custom transactions act as wrappers for the standard ones.

The reason for this approach is that our current authorization concept is not fully refined. As part of our interim solution, we intend to completely block the standard transactions (IQ01 and IQ02) via SM01, and instead allow access only through the custom transactions (ZIQ01 and ZIQ02). This would also simplify role assignments across the organization by enabling us to work solely with the Z-transactions in authorization roles.

To implement this, I used transaction SE93 and tried the following methods:

  1. Creating the custom transactions as copies of the standard ones.

  2. Using the options "Program and screen (dialog transaction)" and "Program and selection screen (report transaction)" (see attached screenshot).

However, none of these approaches worked. Each time, I encounter a system error stating that the new transaction must be maintained in table T370. I have not been able to find any official SAP Note or documentation that explains how to configure this table appropriately.

Could anyone advise on:

  • The correct procedure for creating wrapper transactions for IQ01 and IQ02?

  • Whether configuration of table T370 is mandatory for this purpose, and if so, how it should be maintained?

  • Any SAP Notes or best practices related to this kind of setup?

Thank you very much for your support and guidance.

Best regards,
Osvaldo

entry.title: Re: Forbidden AI Launchpad
entry.content:
Hi Keean,

On the subaccount Security > Users menu option you need to add the ailaunchpad_* roles to the user that you are using. This will solve the issue.

Please see section 5.5 Allow Access to SAP AI Launchpad, page 49: https://help.sap.com/doc/5945759df2d34b69b681c53bb2dd7b9f/CLOUD/en-US/038a6194f65c4ef68885f6f16360dbc4.pdf

Kind regards.

entry.title: SAP Business Data Cloud Data Products and SAP BusinessObjects: Similar Purpose, Different Approach
entry.content:
Today, while navigating through the trial version of SAP Business Data Cloud (BDC) https://lnkd.in/e6sCSy3W, something caught my attention. The concept of Data Products instantly felt very familiar, almost like a modern take on the good old BusinessObjects I’ve worked with before 🤔. 

Like many concepts that get reimagined across SAP's evolving landscape, it got me thinking:

What has actually changed since the launch of BDC Foundation Services and Data Products?
Is it just new branding, or are we looking at a fundamentally different approach to data modeling and consumption?

Let's recap first.

SAP BusinessObjects:

SAP BusinessObjects (SAP BO) is a complete business intelligence (BI) solution. Within a single suite, it enables users to perform OLAP (Online Analytical Processing) and ad-hoc reporting, create predictive dashboards, extract, transform, and load (ETL) data, and create detailed reports similar to those in Crystal Reports.The goal is to conceal the intricacy of the underlying data structure and provide a clear, reusable interface for applications.

Data products in SAP Business Data Cloud :

Data Products represent a modern, cloud-native evolution of the concept of delivering trusted business data. Think of them as governed, consumable data sets with predefined, stable structures and interfaces, designed to be used across a broad range of applications and analytics tools.

 

Some of the key features of Data Products :

  • A Data Product can include master or transactional data.
  • It is produced by a “producer” and consumed by “consumers.”
  • Data Products are optimized for intensive, read-only use.
  • Producers describe their Data Products using Open Resource Discovery (ORD) for easy discoverability.
  • Each Data Product has a clear owner responsible for its lifecycle.
  • Data Products are grouped into Data Packages to organize related datasets.

For example, a Financial Planning Entry Item Data Product could combine multiple related entity types, while a Data Package could group data products related to accounting or cross-application data.

Similarities Between Data Products and BusinessObjects

  • Both provide business-friendly, semantic representations of complex enterprise data.
  • They abstract underlying data structures, making it easier for end-users and applications to consume data.
  • Both support access control and security mechanisms to protect sensitive data.
  • Designed to be reusable and shareable across multiple users and applications.
  • Enable consumption by business intelligence tools, Business Objects via BO clients and dashboards, Data Products via SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC), APIs, and external BI tools.
  • Both aim to deliver trusted, consistent, and meaningful data for decision-making.

Key Differences Between Data Products and BusinessObjects

  • Architecture:
    • Data Products are cloud-native, designed to operate in multi-cloud and hybrid environments as part of a modern data fabric.
    • Business Objects are traditionally on-premises or SAP-hosted, with a focus on structured reporting and workflow integration.
  • Purpose:
    • Data Products focus on data asset management and governed sharing, enabling data mesh principles and broad reuse.
    • Business Objects focuses on reporting, dashboards, and workflow automation within SAP systems.
  • Data Sharing:
    • Data Products are discoverable and consumable by diverse consumers through APIs and tools.
    • Business Objects are mostly shared internally through SAP BO platforms like Web Intelligence, Crystal Reports, or SAP dashboards.
  • Governance and Lineage:
    • Data Products have built-in metadata, certification, and data lineage tracking.
    • Business Objects rely on manual governance and fewer automated lineage features.
  • Consumption:
    • Data Products integrate seamlessly with SAC, APIs, external BI tools, and applications.
    • Business Objects consumption is mainly via BO WebI, Crystal Reports, and SAP dashboarding tools.

When to Use Which?

  • Choose Data Products if you want:
    • A modern, cloud-native data fabric with strong governance
    • Data discoverability and reuse across tools and applications
    • To expose data beyond traditional reporting (APIs, partner apps, etc.)
  • Stick with BusinessObjects if you need:
    • On-premises BI solutions with a rich ecosystem
    • Deep ad-hoc analysis and scheduled reporting
    • Continuity for existing Business Objects clients and workflows

Final Thoughts

SAP Business Data Cloud Data Products and SAP BusinessObjects serve complementary but distinct roles in SAP’s ecosystem. Business Objects remains a powerful tool for classic BI and reporting, while Data Products introduce a new paradigm: treating data as a first-class, governed product ready for cloud-scale analytics and applications.

entry.title: Upgrading from BI 4.3 SP 3 Patch 3 to 4.3 SP4 Patch 7 Issues w/ UV Assoc Dimension Changes
entry.content:
We are upgrading from BI 4.3 SP 3 Patch 3  to  BI 4.3 SP 4 Patch 7. 

We have a Test server/environment for SP 4 Patch 7. We have promoted reports and CMC from our DEV (SP3 Patch 3) environment to the Test environment. 

In some reports, we are seeing issues with the associated dimension in one or more user variables being different in the Test environment.

Some show immediately. For those we've noticed the associated dimension changed from a Merged Dimension to one of the fields within the merge. They can be corrected, and the change is held on saving the report.

For some, the UV looks okay upon opening the report, but on refresh the associated dimension drops off. On retest, we can see that there was a dimension linked, but it goes away on refresh. It can be corrected and will hold on saving he report. 

These issues happen in multiple reports with data from multiple universes. So far there have been 1 or 2 at issue in reports with more than a dozen UVs.

We have more than a thousand reports, so this is not an issue we can move on with the upgrade without resolving. Is this a known issue with Patch 7? Is there a fix for it?

entry.title: S4 Odata call from BTP CAPM application is failing
entry.content:
Hi experts

I have a custom odata call in s/4 system which is called from capm. its a post call configured to be called via destination and cloud connector. it was working fine for almost 2 years until recently where i keep getting teh following error in app log.

message: 'Error during request to remote service: Request failed with status code 403',
code: 'ERR_BAD_REQUEST',
response:
{
status: 403,
statusText: 'Forbidden',
headers: [Object [AxiosHeaders]],
body: 'CSRF token validation failed'
}

Nothing was changed either on the abap side on the odata or the capm side.

destination config is as follows,

"cds": {
        "requires": {
            "OdataApi": {
                "kind": "odata",
                "credentials": {
                    "destination": "DEST",
                    "path": "/sap/opu/odata/SAP/POST_PAY_SRV"
                },
                "csrf": true,
                "csrfInBatch": true
            },

Cloud connector, destination and user all are valid and calling the odata from postman is also working fine. 

Looks like csrf token fetch is not working as expected. (i do call the fetch token in a differnt call in postman and add it to the post call, which then works fine)

I am triggering the post call like below code

 

              const OdataApi = await cds.connect.to("OdataApi");

                const { pay } = OdataApi.entities;

                let payResp = await OdataApi.insert(PayItem).into(pay);

any pointers to solve this is very much appreciated,

thanks

entry.title: Building and Publishing External APIs in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public: From ADT to Postman
entry.content:
Overview

This guide explains how to create and expose a custom external API using the Developer Extensibility approach in SAP S/4HANA Cloud, Public Edition. The process involves creating a custom business object, implementing its behavior, and publishing it as a service accessible to external systems.

 

Prerequisites

  • Access to SAP S/4HANA Cloud, Public Edition with developer extensibility enabled.
  • ABAP Development Tools (ADT) installed in Eclipse.
  • Business role SAP_BR_DEVELOPER assigned.
  • A development package created in the ABAP environment.

Step-by-Step Guide

Create a Custom Database Table: This is the foundational data structure where your application’s data will be stored. It represents the physical storage in the system.

  • In ADT, create a new database table (e.g., ZTODO_LIST).
  • Define required fields and activate the table.

Create a Root CDS View (Interface View): The root CDS view defines the business object’s main entity (the root node) and provides an abstraction over the database table. It enables the use of annotations and is required to define the behavior and expose data services.

  • Right-click your package > New > Data Definition.
  • Name it (e.g., Z_I_TODO_LIST).
  • Use defines root view syntax to expose your database table.
  • Annotate with @ObjectModel: and activate the view.

Create Behavior Definition with Managed Implementation: The behavior definition governs how the data can be interacted with (Create, Read, Update, Delete - CRUD). Using managed implementation reduces manual coding and allows SAP to handle the data persistence layer automatically.

  • Right-click on the CDS view > New Behavior Definition.
  • Mapping the CDS Fields with Custom table.

Create a Projection View: The projection view exposes only the fields and structure you want available to the external world via the API. It acts as the public API interface layered over the root CDS view and enables additional annotations like UI or access control.

  • Right-click > New > Data Definition.
  • Use define root view entity as projection

Create behavior definition for your Projection View: Determines what operations (Create, Update, Delete, Action, etc.) are exposed to consumers of the API.

Define a Service Definition: The service definition tells SAP which projection view (and hence, which data and operations) to expose as a service. This is a required layer before service binding can be created.

  • Right-click > New > Service Definition.

Create a Service Binding: The binding connects the service definition to a specific protocol (e.g., OData V4) and exposes the service endpoint URL.
It also allows you to publish the service so it's available in the system catalog.

  • Right-click > New > Service Binding.
  • Choose OData V4 as the binding type.
  • Name and activate the binding.
  • Click Publish to make it available.

Create a Communication Scenario: The communication scenario defines the APIs and authorizations needed for external communication. It acts as a container that external systems will use to discover and authenticate with your custom service.

  • In ADT, create a communication scenario.
  • Add your service binding.
  • Define inbound service, I/O structure, and authorization.
  • Activate and publish it.

Configure Communication Arrangement in SAP Fiori:  The communication arrangement ties the communication scenario to an actual communication system and user credentials.

Prerequisites:

  1. Create a New Communication User:
    1. Go to the Fiori Launchpad.
    2. Search for or open "Maintain Communication Users" app.
    3. Click on "New".
    4. Fill in the required fields
    5. Save the user.
  2. Create a Communication System:
    1. Go to your SAP Fiori Launchpad.
    2. Search for and open "Communication Systems".
    3. Users for Inbound Communication
    4. Click "New".
    5. Fill Communication Users (technical credentials).
    6. Save.
  • Open “Communication Arrangements” app in SAP Fiori Launchpad.
  • Create a new arrangement using your communication scenario.
  • Set communication system, user, and authentication method.
  • Save to generate the external API URL.

 

Test - Postman:

Conclusion

Building an external API in SAP S/4HANA Cloud (Public Edition) using Developer Extensibility gives developers the tools to connect seamlessly with external systems — securely, efficiently, and in line with SAP best practices. By following a structured, standards-compliant process, you create scalable, flexible APIs that support modern integration needs, third-party tools, and future innovations in the cloud landscape.

And hey — if your API doesn’t work the first time... don’t panic. Just turn it off, activate it again, and pretend it was a test all along. 😅

entry.title: Re: Calculo SDI con absentismos bimestre anterior- “HCM P...
entry.content:
Hola Luis! Buen día!

El asunto se debe a que SAP, por estándar , no trae al presente las diferencias retroactivas en días. Sólo trae el presente las diferencias en importes, siempre y cuando los conceptos de nómina no tengan splits. Esto a través de la función 9LRET.

Entonces, si registras ausentismos e incapacidades en el pasado, el ajuste de esos días se quedará en el pasado (aunque en el presente si verás los ajustes que corresponden a los importes).

De hecho, si para el caso que describes, recalculas el SDI del 1 de marzo, después del retroactivo, verás que el CALC te va a deteminar un SDI variable diferente al que ya tienes para ese día en el IT0372. Esto, porque, debido a que los ajustes en retro para días se quedan en el pasado, para el 1er bimestre tendrás 57 días en vez de los 59 que originalmente se consideraron para el SDI variable. 

Si quieres traer al presente las diferencias en días, puedes seguir las recomendaciones de la nota 578715 

Esto es conveniente para ausentismos, pero no tanto para incapacidades; ya que éstas últimas cuentan con el soporte de una boleta oficial del Instituto que no se deberían alterar en sus fechas de vigencia.

Espero esto te sea de utilidad.

Saludos!

entry.title: Allow user to switch units ($, K, M) in a story
entry.content:
I'd like to provide a radio button or buttons ($,$K,$M) in a story that will help user to switch units on any visible table on the page or perhaps apply to all tables in the story. 

While the script below can explicitly do that for a specific table e.g. tblAcctForecast below. How can I make this generic so that it can work for any table visible on the page/screen or all tables within the story?

var x = RadioButtonGroup_1.getSelectedKey();
 
if (x === "@Thousands") {
	tblAcctForecast.getNumberFormat().setScaleUnit(NumberFormatScaleUnit.Thousand);
	tblAcctForecast.getNumberFormat().setDecimalPlaces(0);
}
else if (x === "@Millions"){
	
	tblAcctForecast.getNumberFormat().setScaleUnit(NumberFormatScaleUnit.Million);
	tblAcctForecast.getNumberFormat().setDecimalPlaces(1);
}
else if (x === "@$"){
	tblAcctForecast.getNumberFormat().setScaleUnit(NumberFormatScaleUnit.Unformatted);
	tblAcctForecast.getNumberFormat().setDecimalPlaces(0);
}

entry.title: DME file format HDFC bank
entry.content:
Hi SAP Guru,

I am configuring DME for XXX bank, India.

Bank has asked for certain fields

like

1.  record identifier - "~"

2.payment run date

3. Identification

4. payment document number

5. amount

6. Payment method

and below that for payment advice it needed

1. advice header - "~H"

2. Payment document number

3. Detail identifier - "~D"

4.Invoice number

5. Invoice amount

With the help of DME i have configured every single data required by the bank

But they require data of payment advice once the data of all the payment document is completed

to explain a bit more I will take e.g. of a pay run

which creates two payment document 200000393 & 200000394

so the output they require is like this

1. reference" ~ "

2.Run ID - 05.04.2013

3. Identification - test

4. Payment document - 200000393 ( it is paid after clearing two invoices 0100000014 )

5. Amount -970

6. payment method - N

1. reference" ~ "

2.Run ID - 05.04.2013

3. Identification - test

4. Payment document - 200000394 ( it is paid after clearing invoices 0100000016 )

5. Amount -975

6. payment method - N

1. ~H

2.Payment document - 200000393

3.~D

4. Invoice number-0100000014

5. Invoice amt - 970

1. ~H

2.Payment document - 200000394

3.~D

4. Invoice number-0100000016

5. Invoice amt - 975

Means all the payment advice data comes after all the part 1 data is finished

for this i have created two separate segments

part1 data is caputred in level1

and payment advice in level 2

so to enable this same I am trying to use delay output in DME segment but dont know how to use it exactly

Pls guide me how can we achieve the same

for your reference I am attaching you the format shared by bank along with sample data shared by the bank

we are on SAP R/3 4.7

entry.title: How Enterprise Architects Can Unlock Strategic Value with SAP Signavio in SAP Landscapes
entry.content:
Author's Note

As a Global Enterprise Architect part of the Enterprise Architecture Advisory at SAP, I've been frequently asked how Enterprise Architects can help in SAP integrated toolchain Signavio, LeanIX, and WalkMe. This blog will deep dive on how Enterprise Architects can bridge the gap and unlock strategic value with SAP Signavio in SAP landscapes, which is also my Part of PhD topic of "Bridging between Enterprise Architects and Business Stakeholders.

Introduction

In today's rapidly evolving business landscape, Enterprise Architects face unprecedented pressure to drive organizational transformation, enhance operational efficiency, and deliver exceptional business outcomes. As the strategic bridge between business vision and technological implementation, Enterprise Architects must navigate complex SAP landscapes while ensuring alignment across the enterprise. This is where SAP Signavio emerges as a powerful tool, offering Enterprise Architects the capabilities needed to accelerate and de-risk transformation initiatives. This blog explores how Enterprise Architects can leverage SAP Signavio to unlock strategic value in SAP landscapes.

The Enterprise Architect's Transformation Challenge

Business transformation is rarely straightforward. Many Enterprise Architects embark on ambitious transformation journeys only to encounter significant challenges along the way. These challenges often stem from a fundamental disconnect between business strategy and execution, siloed operations, and the absence of a common language between business stakeholders and IT teams.

The reality is that successful transformation doesn't depend on a single factor but requires Enterprise Architects to orchestrate alignment across four critical dimensions: strategy, processes, systems, and people. For architects working within SAP landscapes, SAP Signavio addresses this multifaceted challenge by serving as the essential bridge between enterprise vision and technological implementation.

The Four Pillars of Successful Transformation

At the heart of SAP Signavio's approach lies the recognition that transformation success depends on the harmonious integration of four key elements from Strategy to Execution

 

Strategy: Every architectural transformation begins with clearly defined business targets and strategic objectives. Enterprise Architects must translate these high-level goals into specific, actionable business goals. SAP Signavio facilitates this translation through value driver trees that create a clear understanding of where architects should focus their transformation efforts.

For Enterprise Architects, this means connecting what the organization wants to achieve with specific architectural decisions. It specifies targets and business outcomes that architectural changes must support. Every business goal or value driver is impacted by particular business processes, which in turn are supported by architectural components. To improve the architecture in scope, architects must start with analyzing the current state (As-Is). Insights into actual process performance metrics reveal where processes are running well or where architectural bottlenecks exist. This allows Enterprise Architects to validate and prioritize architectural value opportunities.

Processes: Once strategic objectives are established, Enterprise Architects must identify and enhance the business processes that will deliver the desired outcomes. This involves starting with analyzing current process performance, identifying architectural bottlenecks, then continuing to enhance and redesign processes to align with strategic goals.

For Enterprise Architects, process architecture serves as the critical link between business strategy and system implementation. The process designs they create or enhance serve as essential input during solution design, build, test, and deploy phases in the Systems Pillar. By leveraging SAP Signavio, architects can ensure that process designs are value-adding, efficient, reliable, and scalable across the SAP landscape.

Systems: Technology architecture serves as the enabler of process excellence. Enterprise Architects must leverage innovation and automation capabilities to implement processes efficiently and effectively across the SAP landscape.

SAP Signavio ensures that architectural process designs serve as valuable input during solution design, build, test, and deployment phases. This creates a seamless connection between process architecture and system architecture, allowing Enterprise Architects to ensure that SAP implementations truly support business needs.

People: At the center of any architectural transformation are the people who must adopt and execute the new processes and systems. Enterprise Architects must foster collaboration, break down barriers between departments, and help translate business strategy into practical execution that resonates with employees at all levels.

SAP Signavio provides Enterprise Architects with the tools to make transformation human-centric, ensuring that architectural decisions consider the people who will ultimately use the systems and follow the processes.

How Enterprise Architects Can Leverage the SAP Signavio Process Transformation Suite

Enterprise Architects can utilize SAP Signavio's comprehensive suite of tools to support end-to-end architectural transformation. Let's explore how architects can leverage key components for strategic value:

Understanding Your Process Architecture

Before Enterprise Architects can improve process architecture, they need to understand how processes currently operate across the SAP landscape. SAP Signavio provides two powerful tools for architectural analysis:

SAP Signavio Process Insights AND SAP Signavio Process Intelligence

 

 

SAP Signavio Process Insights:  

It enables Enterprise Architects to gain immediate visibility into process performance across SAP landscapes. This allows architects to:

- Rapidly identify architectural improvement opportunities by connecting to existing SAP ERP ECC 6.0 or SAP S/4HANA systems

- Benchmark process performance across different organizational units to identify architectural best practices

- Understand root causes of process inefficiencies that may stem from architectural decisions

- Identify where standardization opportunities exist across the enterprise

- Prioritize architectural interventions based on data-driven insights rather than assumptions

For example, an Enterprise Architect might discover that one regional entity's procure-to-pay process outperforms others due to specific architectural choices. Process Insights helps identify these architectural success patterns, allowing architects to replicate best practices across the enterprise.

 

This SAP Signavio Process Insights powerful drill-down capabilities enable users to understand root causes of process inefficiencies. For example, if one regional entity outperforms others, Process Insights can help identify the reasons behind this success, allowing organizations to replicate best practices across the enterprise. Moreover, it doesn't just highlight problems it provides concrete correction and innovation recommendations to start improving immediately.

SAP Signavio Process Intelligence

Empowers Enterprise Architects to gain granular insights into end-to-end process reality across complex landscapes. While Process Insights focuses on SAP systems, Process Intelligence allows architects to:

- Acquire data from both SAP and non-SAP systems, providing a comprehensive view of processes that span multiple applications

- Visualize how processes actually flow across system boundaries, often revealing architectural disconnects

- Identify integration points that create bottlenecks or compliance issues

- Analyze process variants to inform architectural standardization decisions

- Monitor architectural compliance to ensure implementations match design

Enterprise Architects can use these insights to make informed decisions about application rationalization, integration architecture, and system consolidation strategies.

 

 

Its sophisticated analytics and visualization capabilities, including the famous "spaghetti diagrams," reveal how processes actually run in practice, often differing significantly from theoretical designs. Process Intelligence allows organizations to analyze process performance, monitor compliance levels, and conduct tailored in-depth analyses to address specific business questions.

 

Improving Your Process Architecture

Once Enterprise Architects understand the current state, SAP Signavio provides tools to redesign and enhance process architecture:

SAP Signavio Process Manager AND SAP Signavio Journey Modeler

 

SAP Signavio Process Manager  

Serves as a collaborative modeling environment where Enterprise Architects can document and redesign processes together with business and IT stakeholders. This allows architects to:

- Create standardized process models using BPMN 2.0 that serve as architectural blueprints

- Facilitate "quick modeling" during architectural workshops to capture process requirements

- Ensure architectural consistency through configurable modeling conventions

- Simulate process changes to test architectural impacts before implementation

- Document architectural decisions and rationales for future reference

For Enterprise Architects, the "quick modeling" feature proves invaluable during architectural workshops, where alignment between business requirements and system capabilities is crucial. This capability helps architects rapidly translate business needs into architectural designs that can guide SAP implementations.

 

One particularly valuable feature in Signavio Process Manager  is "quick modeling," which allows consultants to easily capture process discussions during workshops with business stakeholders. This capability proves invaluable during FitGap or Fit2Standard workshops, where alignment between business requirements and system capabilities is crucial.

Process Manager also offers configurable modeling conventions, comprehensive documentation capabilities, and process simulation features that help organizations test the impact of process changes before implementation. 

SAP Signavio Journey Modeler

Extends architectural thinking beyond internal operations to encompass customer, employee, and supplier experiences. Enterprise Architects can:

- Connect experience architecture with process architecture

- Ensure that architectural decisions enhance rather than detract from key experiences

- Identify opportunities to differentiate through experience-led architecture

- Align technical capabilities with experience requirements

- Create a holistic view of how architecture impacts stakeholder journeys

By connecting experiential data with operational processes, Enterprise Architects can ensure that technical architecture decisions support superior experiences for all stakeholders.

 

The Journey Modeler connects experiential data with operational processes, integrates with the business repository, and allows for the reuse of domain objects for personas and touchpoints. This holistic approach ensures that process improvements translate into enhanced experiences for all stakeholders.

 

Executing Architectural Change

Designing improved process architectures is only valuable if those designs can be effectively implemented. SAP Signavio facilitates architectural execution through:

SAP Signavio Process Governance  AND SAP Build Process Automation

 

 

SAP Signavio Process Governance

Helps Enterprise Architects manage architectural change by:

- Establishing governance frameworks for architectural changes

- Assessing architectural maturity across the organization

- Integrating risk and control management into architectural decisions

- Creating configurable workflows for architectural approvals and implementations

This governance capability ensures that architectural standards are maintained as changes are implemented across the SAP landscape.

 

 

SAP Build Process Automation

Empowers Enterprise Architects to bridge gaps in the application landscape by:

- Automating cross-system processes that span architectural boundaries

- Creating forms and workflows that connect disparate systems

- Implementing architectural quick wins through low-code automation

- Addressing integration challenges without extensive custom development

This capability allows Enterprise Architects to implement architectural improvements rapidly, demonstrating value while larger transformation initiatives are underway.

 

 

Fostering Architectural Collaboration

Enterprise Architecture is inherently collaborative, requiring input and buy-in from stakeholders across the organization. SAP Signavio enhances architectural collaboration through:

SAP Signavio Process Collaboration Hub

Provides Enterprise Architects with a platform to:

- Create a single source of truth for process architecture

- Share architectural insights and standards across the organization

- Guide implementation teams with clear architectural direction

- Increase transparency of architectural decisions and rationales

- Foster cross-functional understanding of how processes and systems interconnect

 

The Collaboration Hub transforms architectural artifacts from static documentation into living assets that drive organizational behavior. By making architecture accessible and understandable to all stakeholders, Enterprise Architects can increase adoption and ensure that transformation efforts translate into actual operational changes.

 

Accelerating Architectural Transformation

Enterprise Architects can accelerate transformation through pre-built content and best practices:

SAP Signavio Process Explorer

serves as the gateway to a vast repository of architectural accelerators, including:

- Reference architectures for common business scenarios

- Industry-specific process models that can inform architectural decisions

- Best practice content that aligns with SAP standard capabilities

- Metrics and benchmarks to guide architectural priorities

For example, during an SAP S/4HANA implementation, Enterprise Architects can use SAP best practice content as the foundation for their architectural designs, significantly reducing the time and effort required for architecture definition while ensuring alignment with SAP capabilities.

 

 

What are SAP Signavio Value Accelerators?

Are content packages for either a process or an industry that contain one or more of the following assets: 

  • Best-practice process and journey models 
  • Business and solutions capability maps 
  • Benchmarks, metrics and dashboards 
  • SAP solutions recommendations 
  • Thought leadership papers, ‘how-to’ manuals and videos  
  • Connectors, integrations, data transformation templates and other technical enablers 

 


SAP Signavio Value Accelerators provide customers with foundational templates and an optimal path to follow, avoiding them the complexity, costs and risks to start from a blank page. No matter if organizations are transforming an existing process, starting a new business model or going through a large IT transformation (such as SAP S/4HANA), SAP Signavio Value Accelerators bundle together all of the ingredients for a successful project.  

 

Strategic Use Cases for Enterprise Architects

Enterprise Architects can leverage SAP Signavio across a wide range of strategic scenarios:

SAP S/4HANA Transformation: When leading S/4HANA implementations, Enterprise Architects can use SAP Signavio to:

- Document current-state process architecture to identify simplification opportunities

- Design future-state processes that leverage S/4HANA capabilities

- Identify process standardization opportunities across business units

- Prioritize implementation phases based on business value

- Ensure architectural decisions support strategic objectives

Application Portfolio Rationalization: Enterprise Architects using LeanIX tasked with consolidating applications can:

- Map processes across multiple systems to identify redundancies

- Analyze process variations to determine standardization potential

- Design harmonized processes for consolidated landscapes

- Quantify the business impact of rationalization decisions

- Create a roadmap for phased consolidation

Enterprise-Wide Process Standardization: Architects driving standardization initiatives can:

- Discover process variants across organizational units

- Identify internal best practices based on performance data

- Design standardized processes that incorporate proven approaches

- Create governance frameworks to maintain standards

- Measure the impact of standardization on business outcomes

Continuous Architecture Evolution: After initial transformation projects, Enterprise Architects can:

- Monitor process performance to identify new architectural improvement opportunities

- Implement incremental enhancements to optimize the landscape

- Evaluate new SAP innovations for potential architectural impact

- Maintain architectural alignment with evolving business needs

- Quantify the ongoing value of architectural decisions

 

The Strategic Value for Enterprise Architects

For Enterprise Architects working in SAP landscapes, SAP Signavio delivers unique strategic value:

Business-IT Alignment: By providing a common language and collaborative platform, SAP Signavio helps Enterprise Architects bridge the traditional gap between business and IT stakeholders, ensuring that architectural decisions truly support business needs.

Data-Driven Architecture: Rather than relying on assumptions, Enterprise Architects can make architectural decisions based on actual process performance data, increasing the likelihood of successful outcomes.

Accelerated Time-to-Value: The combination of pre-built content, collaborative tools, and rapid analysis capabilities allows Enterprise Architects to deliver value faster, reducing the time from architectural concept to business impact.

Reduced Transformation Risk: By providing visibility into process reality and enabling simulation of changes, SAP Signavio helps Enterprise Architects identify and mitigate risks before they impact implementation.

Sustainable Governance: The governance capabilities ensure that architectural standards are maintained over time, preventing the gradual erosion of architectural integrity that often occurs after initial implementations.

Conclusion

In an era where business transformation has become a continuous necessity rather than a one-time event, Enterprise Architects need powerful tools to navigate complex SAP landscapes while delivering strategic value. SAP Signavio provides the comprehensive capabilities architects need to understand current processes, design future states, implement changes, and govern ongoing evolution.

By leveraging SAP Signavio, Enterprise Architects can truly serve as the bridge between business strategy and technological implementation, ensuring that SAP investments deliver maximum value to the organization. Whether you're leading a major S/4HANA transformation, harmonizing processes across your enterprise, or pursuing continuous architectural improvement, SAP Signavio offers the insights, capabilities, and support you need to achieve your strategic objectives.

As organizations continue to face pressure to transform rapidly and effectively, Enterprise Architects equipped with SAP Signavio can become indispensable allies in the journey toward operational excellence and business success.

Final Thoughts

The role of Enterprise Architects continues to evolve in our increasingly complex digital landscape. We are no longer just technical specialists but strategic partners who must speak both the language of business and technology. In my experience working with organizations across various industries, I've found that the most successful Enterprise Architects are those who can effectively translate business needs into architectural solutions while maintaining a clear line of sight to business outcomes.

SAP Signavio represents a significant step forward in empowering Enterprise Architects to fulfill this critical bridging function. By providing data-driven insights, collaborative tools, and a common language for business and IT stakeholders, it enables architects to move beyond documentation to become true agents of transformation.

 

Ramy Salem
ANZ Toolchain Ambassador at SAP
PhD Candidate – AI Agents for Enterprise Architecture
Certified in SAP | WalkMe | LeanIX | Signavio

entry.title: Re: Fondo de ahorro MX, devolución del descuento en retro...
entry.content:
Hola!

Un comentario adicional a lo que ya sugerí: verifiquen si utilizan la misma base de beneficios para Fondo de Ahorro y para Vales de Despensa. Es común usar la misma base cuando ambos beneficios se calculan en base a un porcentaje. 

Por estándar, la base para ambos es el cc-n /102. 

Entonces, si siguen las recomendaciones que les hice, y ambos beneficios utilizan la misma base, entonces lo que hagan con el /102 afectará a ambas prestaciones.

Para verificar qué base están utilizando deben acceder a la vista V_5UBA_WT. Aquí una imagen:

 Vista V_5UBA_WT para F.Ahorro 

Espero consigan resolver este tema.

Saludos!
