entry.content: Hi Ryan-Crosby,

entry.title: Flexible Workflow for Purchase Order in S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition
entry.content:
Environment 

  • SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition
  • SAP Central Business Configuration

This Blog covers five topics:

  • Configure Flexible Workflow for Purchase Order
  • Rework Flexible Workflow for Purchase Order
  • Purchase Order Workflow Review
  • Custom Attributes in Task Cards for Purchase Order
  • Ad-hoc Approver in Purchase Order Workflow
  • Appendix: SAP Knowledge Base Article for Workflow and Workflow App Components

1: Configure Flexible Workflow for Purchase Order

1.1Purpose

This document describes how to optimize the PO approval process by using App Manage Workflow for Purchase Orders. The flexible workflow allows you to use either the automatic, one-step, or even multi-step approval.

You can do the following with App Manage Workflows for Purchase Orders:

  • Determine the approver of the workflow, for example, the manager of the last approver, or directly assign a specific user
  • Define start conditions for a workflow and for each approval step, and the order in which the start conditions of existing workflows are checked
  • Add new steps to a workflow at any time
  • Add new workflows, copy existing ones or modify them according to your requirements.

Business Background (For Example):

A group company has two subsidiary companies, one is company 1010, and another is company 1020.

For company 1010, all purchase orders should have the approval process. No approval process is needed for company 1020.

  • Total net amount of purchase order is smaller than or equal to 500 EUR, then the PO needs the approval of the purchasing manager and the project manager.
  • Total net amount of purchase order is greater than 500 EUR and smaller than or equal to 6000 EUR, then the PO needs to be approved by the purchasing manager, project manager, and the resource manager.
  • Total net amount of purchase order is greater than 6000 EUR and smaller than or equal to 150,000 EUR, then the PO needs to be approved by purchasing manager and project manager, resource manager and manager.
  • Total net amount of purchase order is greater than 150,000 EUR and smaller than or equal to 400,000 EUR, then the PO needs to be approved by purchasing manager, project manager, resource manager, manager, and VP.
  • Total net amount of purchase order is greater than 400,000 EUR, then the PO needs to be approved by purchasing manager, project manager, resource manager, manager, VP, and President.

The following release condition for purchasing approval is carried out for Company Code 1010:

1.2 Overview Table

The configure Flexible workflow for purchase order consists of the process step, as provided in the table below.

Step

App

Business Role

Configure Flexible Workflow for Purchase Order

Manage Workflows for Purchase Orders (F2872)

SAP_BR_ BPC_EXPERT

1.3 Procedure

Test Step

Test Step Name

Instruction

1

Log On

Log onto the SAP Fiori launchpad using the Configuration Expert role.

2

Access App

Open Manage Workflows for Purchase Orders.

3

Check Automatic Release of Purchase Order

In Workflows list, choose Automatic Release of Purchase Order, make sure workflow Automatic Release of Purchase Order is in active status.

！Note

The Automatic Release of Purchase Order is delivered by SAP. By default, this workflow is activated.

4

Create Flexible Workflow

On the screen Manage Workflows, choose Create to create Flexible Workflow.

 

Create step 1

On the New Workflow screen, make the following entries:

Name: Test Workflow for Purchase Order

In the Start Conditions area, select Company Code of purchase order is 1010.  

In Steps area and Workflow Steps choose Create.                                         

On the New Step screen,  make the following entries:

Step Name : PO 0-500 EUR

Type: Release Purchase Order

Go to the Recipients area:

Assignment By: User

User: Select User with Purchasing_manager from F4 help.

Step to be completed by: One of the recipients.

In the Step Conditions area, choose Total net amount of purchase order is greater than 0 EUR

Choose Create and go back to the New Workflow screen.

 

Create step 2

In Steps area and Workflow Steps, choose Create.                                        

On the New Step screen,  make the following entries:

Name : PO 0-500 EUR

Type: Release Purchase Order

Go to the Recipients area:

Assignment By: User

User: Select User with PROJECT_MANAGER from F4 help.

Step to be completed by: One of the recipients.

In the Step Conditions area, choose Total net amount of purchase order is greater than 0 EUR

Choose Create and go back to the New Workflow screen.

 

Create step 3

In Steps area and Workflow Steps, choose Create                                         

On the New Step screen,  make the following entries:

Name : PO 501-6000 EUR

Type: Release Purchase Order

Go to the Recipients area:

Assignment By: User

User: Select User with RESOURCE_MANAGER from F4 help.

Step to be completed by: One of the recipients.

In the Step Conditions area, choose Total net amount of purchase order is greater than 500 EUR

Choose Create and go back to the New Workflow screen.

 

Create step 4

In Steps area and Workflow Steps, choose Create,                                        

On the New Step screen,  make the following entries:

Name : PO 6001-150000 EUR

Type: Release Purchase Order

Go to the Recipients area:

Assignment By: User

User: Select User with Manager John from F4 help.

Step to be completed by: One of the recipients.

In the Step Conditions area, choose Total net amount of purchase order is greater than 6000 EUR

Choose Create and go back to the New Workflow screen.

 

Create step 5

In Steps area and Workflow Steps, choose Create                                         

On the New Step screen,  make the following entries:

Name : PO 150001-400000 EUR

Type: Release Purchase Order

Go to the Recipients area:

Assignment By: User

User: Select User with SAPVP from F4 help.

Step to be completed by: One of the recipients.

In the Step Conditions area, choose Total net amount of purchase order is greater than 150000 EUR

Choose Create and go back to the New Workflow screen.

 

Create step 6

In Steps area and Workflow Steps, choose Create,                                        

On the New Step screen,  make the following entries:

Name : PO > 400000 EUR

Type: Release Purchase Order

Go to the Recipients area:

Assignment By: User

User: Select User with SAPPRESIDENT from F4 help.

Step to be completed by: One of the recipients.

In the Step Conditions area, choose Total net amount of purchase order is greater than 400000 EUR

Choose Create and go back to the New Workflow screen.

5

Save Manage Workflow

Choose Save.

6

Activate Workflow Item

Choose Activate on the top right of the screen.

！ Note

  1. Make sure each approval users have assigned business role SAP_BR_PURCHASING_MANAGER to have the PO approval authorization.
  2. The Purchase Order approval must be an employee user.
  3. When defining the recipients for a workflow step (in the app Manage Workflows for Purchase Orders), you can select the following agent determination rules, for example:
  • Manager of Workflow Initiator
  • Manager of Workflow Initiator's Manager
  • Manager of Last Approver

To ensure that these rules work, you must define the relationships of managers and employees.

2: Rework Flexible Workflow for Purchase Orders

2.1 Overview Table

The rework flexible workflow for purchase orders consists of several process steps, as provided in the table below.

Step

App

Business Role

Activate Inquiry Workflow for Purchase Orders

Manage Your Solution (F1241)

SAP_BR_ BPC_EXPERT

Configure Rework Flexible Workflow for Purchase Orders

Manage Workflows for Purchase Orders (F2872)

SAP_BR_ BPC_EXPERT

Rework Purchase Order

My Inbox - All Items (F026)

SAP_BR_PURCHASING_MANAGER

 

2.2 Procedure

2.2.1 Activate Inquiry Workflow for Purchase Orders

Purpose

In this procedure, you activate the Inquiry Workflow for Purchase Orders. This provides approvers with an additional option to send back purchase orders to purchase order creators for rework. For items subject to this workflow, approvers have an additional option Request Rework in the My Inbox app. Approvers can choose this option and request the purchase order creator to make some changes in the purchase order.

Procedure

Test Step

Test Step Name

Instruction

1

Log On

Log on to the SAP Fiori Launchpad as a Configuration Expert - Business Process Configuration.

2

Access App

Open Manage Your Solution (F1241).

3

Open Configure Your Solution

Choose Configure Your Solution.

Note To create country-region-dependent settings for the intended local version, choose Set Local Version.

4

Open Activate Inquiry Workflow for Purchase Orders

On the Configure Your Solution: Realize Phase screen, search Activate Inquiry Workflow for Purchase Orders, then choose Search.

Select the line of the item and go to the next screen.

On the Configure Your Solution - Purchase Order Processing screen, choose Configure on the line of configuration step with name Activate Inquiry Workflow for Purchase Orders.

5

Activate Inquiry Workflow for Purchase Orders

Choose the Change State to change Switch State from OFF to On.

2.2.2 Configure Rework Flexible Workflow for Purchase Orders

Procedure

Test Step

Test Step Name

Instruction

1

Log On

Log on to the SAP Fiori launchpad as a Configuration Expert - Business Process Configuration.

2

Access the App

Open Manage Workflows for Purchase Orders (F2872).

3

Create Manage Workflow

Choose Create, and make the following entries:

Workflow Name: <Test Rework Workflow for Purchase Order>

From the Start Conditions section, add the following preconditions:

Purchasing group of purchase order is: Selected

Purchasing Group: 003

Choose Create another condition, and make the following entries:

Total net amount of purchase order is greater than: Selected

Amount: For example, 500,00

Currency: EUR

In the Steps area, choose Create and make the following entries:

Step Type: Release of Reworkable Purchase Order

In the Recipients area, make the following entries:

Assignment By: User

User: Select User from value help (with Employee ID PURCHASING_MANAGER)

Choose OK.

Step to be completed by: One of the recipients.

Exception Handling:

Rework Requested:

Required Action: Rework Purchase Order

Recipients: Default: <Role: Determined automatically>

Action result: Repeat step

 

!Note Action result should be (after rework):

● Continue Workflow: Current Workflow shall move to next step

● Repeat Step: repeat the current step of current workflow

● Restart workflow: restart the workflow

 

Choose Create.

Choose Save.

4

Activate Workflow Item

Select the Workflow Item just created, and choose Activate.

2.2.3 Rework Purchase Order

Procedure

Test Step

Test Step Name

Instruction

1

Log On

Log on to the SAP Fiori launchpad as a Purchasing Manager.

 

2

Access the App

Open My Inbox - All Items (F0826) .

3

Rework Purchase Order

Select the Purchase Order from the left screen and choose Request Rework at the bottom right of the screen.

Enter the rework reason in the Submit Decision dialog then choose Submit.

4

Back to SAP Fiori launchpad

Choose the Home button to go back to the SAP Fiori launchpad.

After this step, the Purchase Order should be edited by the creator with the rework reason.

!Note: Working with Purchase Orders in My Inbox App, the required Business Catalog is SAP_MM_BC_PO_WIAPPROVE_PC.

3: Purchase Order Workflow Review

3.1 Overview Table

The purchase order workflow review consists of several process steps, as provided in the table below.

Step

App

Business Role

Configure Rework Flexible Workflow for Purchase Orders

Manage Workflows for Purchase Orders (F2872)

SAP_BR_ BPC_EXPERT

Review Purchase Order Items

My Inbox - All Items (F0826)

SAP_BR_PURCHASER

3.2 Procedure

3.2.1 Configure Purchase Order Flexible Workflow for Review

Purpose

In this procedure, you can configure the step Purchase Order Flexible Workflow for Reviewer. In the review steps, you can define the reviewers or people responsible for monitoring the progress of the workflow. When a workflow is started, all the reviewers are notified. The reviewers can then track the progress of the workflow. Workflow items pertaining to review steps can only be monitored, and cannot be approved, rejected, or sent back for rework.

Procedure

Test Step

Test Step Name

Instruction

1

Log on

Log onto the SAP Fiori launchpad using the role Configuration Expert - Business Process Configuration.

2

Access the App

Choose Manage Workflows for Purchase Orders (F2872).

3

Create Manage Workflow

Choose Create, and make the following entry:

Workflow Name: <Test Workflow for Purchase Order>

In section Start Conditions, add the following preconditions:

●      Purchasing group of purchase order is: Selected

●      Purchasing Group: 003

Choose Create another condition, and make the following entries:

●      Total net amount of purchase order is greater than: Selected

●      Amount: For example, 50,000

●      Currency: EUR

In the Steps area, choose Create and make the following entry:

Step Type: Release of Purchase Order

In the Recipients area, make the following entries:

●      Assignment By: User

●      User: Select User from value help (with Employee ID PURCHASING_MANAGER)

Choose OK.

Step to be completed by: One of the recipients.

Choose Create.

In the Review Steps area, choose Create and make the following entries:

●      Step Type: Review Workflow of Purchase Order

●      Assignment By: User

●      User: Select user from value help (with employee ID), for example, PURCHASER.

Choose OK.

Choose Create.

Choose Save.

4

Activate Workflow Item

Select the workflow item you just created and choose Activate.

!Note: Make sure the default workflow Automatic Release of Purchase Order is activated.

3.2.2 Review Purchase Order Items

Purpose

In this procedure, purchase order workflow reviewers can login to system and review purchase order items.

Procedure

Test Step

Test Step Name

Instructions

1

Log On

Log on to the SAP Fiori launchpad as a Purchaser.

2

Access the App

Open My Inbox - All Items (F0826)

3

Review Purchase Order Items

Review the purchase order items. After the purchase order workflow completes, the purchase order items would be invisible in the reviewer's My Inbox.

！Note

If the reviewers don’t have My Inbox App, you need to assign business catalog SAP_MM_BC_PO_WIAPPROVE_PC to business role of the reviewers.

4: Custom Attributes in Task Cards for Purchase Order Workflow

4.1 Overview Table

The custom attributes in task cards for purchase order workflow consists of several process steps, as provided in the table below.

Step

App

Business Role

Maintain CDS Based Custom Attributes for Purchase Order Workflow

Manage Your Solution (F1241)

SAP_BR_ BPC_EXPERT

Approve Purchase Order through Task Cards

My Inbox - All Items (F0826)

SAP_BR_PURCHASING_MANAGER

4.2 Procedure

Purpose

The requirement is to enable the Custom attributes along with workflow task data in task card and in My Inbox with Multi Select mode. Custom attributes allow to expose business-relevant data along with workflow task data. They are usually relevant for taking a decision regarding the task and can be prominently exposed in the Inbox of the user that is processing it.

!Note When you process this step, make sure that you have processed the step Check Flexible Workflow for Purchase Order Approval and Configure Flexible Workflow for Purchase Order in the above Steps.

 

4.2.1 Maintain CDS Based Custom Attributes for Purchase Order Workflow

Purpose

Custom attributes allow the exposure of business-relevant data along with the workflow task data. They are usually relevant for taking a decision regarding the task and can be exposed in the Inbox of the user processing it. Here you can configure a CDS Entity and a CDS Entity Element for the purchase order Flexible Workflow Scenario. During creation of a task instance the configured CDS Entity Element will be used to retrieve the value displayed to the task processor.

Procedure

Test Step

Test Step Name

Instruction

1

Log On

Log on to the SAP Fiori Launchpad as a Configuration Expert - Business Process Configuration.

2

Access App

Open Manage Your Solution (F1241).

3

Open Configure Your Solution

Choose Configure Your Solution.

!Note To create country-/region-dependent settings for the intended local version, choose Set Local Version.

 

4

Open Maintain CDS Based Custom Attributes

On the Configure Your Solution: Realize Phase screen, search Maintain CDS Based Custom Attributes , then choose Search.

Select the line of the item and go to the next screen.

On the Configure Your Solution - Output Control screen, choose Configure on the line of configuration step with name Maintain CDS Based Custom Attributes .

5

Create Custom Attributes (Company Code) for PO Flexible Workflow

Click New Entries, and enter the following entries:

● Task/FlexWorkflow: WS00800238

● Step ID: 19

● Name of Attribute: < COMPANYCODE >

• Active: Select

• Description: <Company Code>

• Rank: 60

● CDS Entity: C_PURORDWORKFLOWTASKCARD

● CDS Entity Element: use F4 to choose COMPANYCODE

Choose Enter.

● XSD Type: <Default Value:xs:string>

● XSD Length: <Default Value:4>

● XSD Decimals: <Default Value:0>

● ABAP type: <Default Value:C Character String>

Choose Save.

6

Create Custom Attributes (Purchase Order Creator) for PO Flexible Workflow

Click New Entries, and enter the following entries:

● Task/FlexWorkflow: WS00800238

● Step ID: 19

● Name of Attribute: < CREATEDBYUSER>

• Active: Select

• Description: <Purchase Order Creator >

• Rank: 40

● CDS Entity: C_PURORDWORKFLOWTASKCARD

● CDS Entity Element: use F4 to choose CREATEDBYUSER

Choose Enter.

● XSD Type: <Default Value:xs:string>

● XSD Length: <Default Value:12>

● XSD Decimals: <Default Value:0>

● ABAP type: <Default Value:C Character String>

Choose Save.

7

Create Custom Attributes (Purchase Order Date) for PO Flexible Workflow

Click New Entries, and enter the following entries:

● Task/FlexWorkflow: WS00800238

● Step ID: 19

● Name of Attribute: < PURCHASEORDERDATE>

• Active: Select

• Description: <Purchase Order Date>

• Rank: 50

● CDS Entity: C_PURORDWORKFLOWTASKCARD

● CDS Entity Element: use F4 to choosePURCHASEORDERDATE

Choose Enter.

● XSD Type: <Default Value:xs:date>

● XSD Length: <Default Value:8>

● XSD Decimals: <Default Value:0>

● ABAP type: <Default Value:D Date (YYYYMMDD)>

Choose Save.

8

Create Custom Attributes (Purchase Order Net Value) for PO Flexible Workflow

Click New Entries, and enter the following entries:

● Task/FlexWorkflow: WS00800238

● Step ID: 19

● Name of Attribute: < PURCHASEORDERNETAMOUNT>

• Active: Select

• Description: <Purchase Order Net Value>

• Rank: 100

● CDS Entity: C_PURORDWORKFLOWTASKCARD

● CDS Entity Element: use F4 to choosePURCHASEORDERNETAMOUNT

Choose Enter.

● XSD Type: <Default Value:xs:decimal>

● XSD Length: <Default Value:23>

● XSD Decimals: <Default Value:2>

● ABAP type: <Default Value:P Packed number>

Choose Save.

9

Create Custom Attributes (Purchase Group) for PO Flexible Workflow

Click New Entries, and enter the following entries:

● Task/FlexWorkflow: WS00800238

● Step ID: 19

● Name of Attribute: < PURCHASINGGROUP>

• Active: Select

• Description: <Purchase Group>

• Rank: 70

● CDS Entity: C_PURORDWORKFLOWTASKCARD

● CDS Entity Element: use F4 to choosePURCHASINGGROUP

Choose Enter.

● XSD Type: <Default Value:xs:string>

● XSD Length: <Default Value:3>

● XSD Decimals: <Default Value:0>

● ABAP type: <Default Value:C Character String>

Choose Save.

10

Create Custom Attributes (Purchasing Organization) for PO Flexible Workflow

Click New Entries, and enter the following entries:

● Task/FlexWorkflow: WS00800238

● Step ID: 19

● Name of Attribute: < PURCHASINGORGANIZATION>

• Active: Select

• Description: <Purchasing Organization>

• Rank: 80

● CDS Entity: C_PURORDWORKFLOWTASKCARD

● CDS Entity Element: use F4 to choosePURCHASINGORGANIZATION

Choose Enter.

● XSD Type: <Default Value:xs:string>

● XSD Length: <Default Value:4>

● XSD Decimals: <Default Value:0>

● ABAP type: <Default Value:C Character String>

Choose Save.

11

Create Custom Attributes (Supplier) for PO Flexible Workflow

Click New Entries, and enter the following entries:

● Task/FlexWorkflow: WS00800238

● Step ID: 19

● Name of Attribute: < Supplier>

• Active: Select

• Description: <Supplier Details>

• Rank: 90

● CDS Entity: C_PURORDWORKFLOWTASKCARD

● CDS Entity Element: use F4 to chooseSUPPLIER

Choose Enter.

● XSD Type: <Default Value:xs:string>

● XSD Length: <Default Value:10>

● XSD Decimals: <Default Value:0>

● ABAP type: <Default Value:C Character String>

Choose Save.

12

Choose Back

Choose Back to return to the previous screen.

13

Choose Save.

Choose Save.

 

4.2.2 Approve Purchase Order through Task Cards

Purpose

! Note: After completing step Maintain CDS based custom attributes for purchase order workflow, you can process this setup, and the step: Maintain CDS based custom attributes for purchase order workflow (Optional) is a prerequisite for this step. You can ignore this step if you did not set up the Steps: Maintain CDS based custom attributes for purchase order workflow.

Procedure

Test Step

Test Step Name

Instruction

1

Log On

Log on to the SAP Fiori launchpad as a Purchasing Manager.

2

Access the App

Open My Inbox - All Items (F0826) .

3

Click Multi Select button

Click on Multi-Select button on left panel.

Select Task Type is Release of Purchase Order. If the Select Task Type pop up screen is not displayed, then you can ignore this step.

The Task Summary has been displayed on the right panel. All the custom attributes should be displayed in table view on right panel along with task based custom attributes.

Sequence should be based on ranking maintained in SSCUI 106664 (Maintain CDS Based Custom Attributes).

4

Select the Purchase Order

Select the purchase order that you want to approve.

5

Choose Approve

Choose Approve at the bottom right.

6

Enter Approval Reason

Enter the approval reason if necessary, and then choose Submit.

 

5: Ad-hoc Approver in Purchase Order Workflow

Purpose

The purpose of this case is to implement the Ad-hoc approver step in approval details of purchase order in App: Manage Purchase Orders.

5.1 Overview Table

The Ad-hoc Approver in the purchase order workflow consists of several process steps, as provided in the table below.

Step

App

Business Role

Configure Flexible Workflow for Purchase Orders

Manage Workflows for Purchase Orders (F2872)

SAP_BR_ BPC_EXPERT

Create Purchase Order to create ad-hoc workflow steps

Manage Purchase Order(F0842A)

SAP_BR_PURCHASER

Approve Purchase Order

My Inbox - All Items (F0826)

SAP_BR_PURCHASING_MANAGER

Approve Purchase Order

My Inbox - All Items (F0826)

SAP_BR_PURCHASER

 

5.2 Procedure

5.2.1 Configure Flexible Workflow for Purchase Orders

Purpose

In this procedure, you configure the flexible workflow for Purchase Order Approval.

!Note：

  • When you start to process this step, please make sure that the flexible workflow for purchase document type NB has been activated in your system.
  • For the Workflow Name and Steps Name, you can translate to other languages via app Workflow Content Translation, for example translate it from English to German.

Procedure

Test Step 

Test Step Name

Instruction

1

Log On

Log on to the SAP Fiori launchpad as a Configuration Expert - Business Process Configuration.

2

Access the App

Open Manage Workflows for Purchase Orders (F2872).

3

Create Manage Workflow

Choose Create, and make the following entries:

Workflow Name: <Test Workflow for Purchase Order>

From the Start Conditions section, add the following preconditions:

Purchasing group of purchase order is: Selected

Purchasing Group: 003

Choose Create another condition, and make the following entries:

Total net amount of purchase order is greater than: Selected

Amount: For example, 500,00

Currency: EUR

In the Steps area, choose Create and make the following entries:

Step Type: Release of Purchase Order

In the Recipients area, make the following entries:

Assignment By: User

User: Select User from value help (with Employee ID PURCHASING_MANAGER)

Choose OK.

Step to be completed by: One of the recipients.

Choose Create.

Choose Save.

4

Activate Workflow Item

Select the Workflow Item just created, and choose Activate.

!Note: Please make sure default workflow Automatic Release of Purchase Order has been activated.

5.2.2 Create Purchase Order to add ad-hoc workflow steps

Purpose

In this procedure, you create a purchase order to add ad-hoc workflow steps

Procedure

Test Step 

Test Step Name

Instruction

1

Log On

Log on to the SAP Fiori launchpad as a Purchaser.

2

Access App

Open Manage Purchase Orders (F0842A).

3

Start Purchase Order Creation

Without making any selection, choose Create.

4

Enter Purchase Order Data

Enter all of the necessary data.

On the General Information tab, make the following entries:

Purchasing Doc. Type: Standard PO (NB)

●      Supplier: 10300001

●      Company Code: 1010

●      Purchasing Organization: 1010

●      Purchasing Group: 003

●      Currency: EUR

On the Items tab, from the Purchase Order Items screen, choose Create and make the following entries:

●      Material: TG0011

●      Plant: 1010

●      Order Quantity: <100>

●      Net Order Price: <30>

Navigate to the selected item by clicking on the Chevron (>) or by directly clicking on the item line.

On the General Information tab, make the following entry:

●      Storage Location: 101A

From the Purchase Order Item section, choose Process Flow tab.

The following entries display:

●      Goods Receipt: yes

●      Invoice Receipt: <selected>

Verify Goods-Receipt-Based Invoice Verification is selected.

On the tab Tax, check the following entries:

●      Tax Code: <Default Value>

●      Tax Date: <Default Value>

On the Schedule Lines tab, make the following entry:

●      Delivery date: <a date in the future>

Choose Apply.

5

Verify Purchase Order for Completeness and Save Purchase Order

Choose Order to save the Purchase Order.

6

Go to Approve Details to edit

Navigate to the Approve Details tab, choose Edit to go to the Workflow screen.

7

Add the ad-hoc approver

Choose Create and make the following entries:

Step Type: Release of Purchase Order

 In the Workflow Steps area, choose Create and make the following entries:

Step Type: Release of Purchase Order

In the Recipients area, make the following entries:

Assignment By: User

User: Select User from value help (with Employee ID PURCHASER)

Choose Create.

! Note: Ensure that you are assigned the business catalog SAP_MM_BC_PO_ADHOCAPPROVE_PC.

Please contact your system administrator for required authorization.

 

8

Choose Save

Go to the Workflow screen, and choose Save.

 

9

Choose Order

Choose Order to save the Purchase Order.

!Note

  • The Edit button is relevant only if the purchase orders are in approval or if the workflow has ended in error.
  • If a purchase order with ad-hoc workflow steps is copied, the copy will not have the ad-hoc steps.
  • The ad-hoc steps do not affect the workflow configuration in the Manage Workflows for Purchase Orders app.
  • Only planned steps can be modified.

5.2.3 Approve Purchase Order

Purpose

In this procedure, purchaser manager can approve the purchase order accordingly.

Procedure

Test Step

Test Step Name

Instruction

1

Log On

Log on to the SAP Fiori launchpad as a Purchasing Manager.

2

Access the App

Open My Inbox - All Items (F0826).

3

Search for Purchase Order Status

Make the following entry and choose Enter:

●      Search: <Purchase Order number>

4

Approve Purchase Order

Select the Purchase Order on the left side of the screen and choose Approve at the bottom right.

5

Enter Approval Reason

Enter the approval reason if necessary, and then choose Submit.

Log on to the SAP Fiori launchpad as a purchaser and repeat the above step for approving purchase order.

6: Appendix

6.1 SAP Knowledge Base Article for Workflow

1: KBA: 2587036 - Edit workflow Settings in Purchasing Configuration

https://me.sap.com/notes/2587036/E

2: KBA: 2751275 - Workflow Configuration Guide for Purchasing Documents

https://me.sap.com/notes/2751275/E

3: KBA: 2767845 - How to define new Procurement Pre-conditions in Manage Workflow Apps - SAP S/4HANA and SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition

https://me.sap.com/notes/2767845/E

4: KBA: 2824240 - No users available in People picker 

https://me.sap.com/notes/2824240/E

5: KBA: 3053155 - Restart Purchase Order Workflow using SSCUI 103345 and BAdI MMPUR_PO_WORKFLOW_RESTAR

https://me.sap.com/notes/3053155/E

6: KBA: 3213204 - Workflow data details for Purchase Orders, Purchase requisitions, Supplier Invoice

https://me.sap.com/notes/3213204/E

6.2 PO Workflow App Components

 App Name

Component

Component Description

Manage Workflows for Purchase Orders (F2872)

MM-FIO-PUR-PO-WFL

Purchase Order Workflow

My Inbox - All Items (F0862)

CA-INB-FIO

Fiori UI for My Inbox Application

Manage Purchase Orders (F0842A)

MM-FIO-PUR-PO

Fiori UI for Purchase Orders

entry.title: Error "Rule Service with given ID does not exist" when Consuming Decision Table API(SBPA)
entry.content:
Hi Team,

I have created a decision table in SAP Build Process Automation and have successfully released and deployed the project. Following this, I created a trigger from the deployed decision table.

However, when I try to consume this API via Postman, I encounter the following error message:

"The rule service with given id xxxxxxxxxxxxx does not exist."

Has anyone come across this error before? Any insights or suggestions to resolve this would be greatly appreciated.

Thank you in advance for your help.

entry.title: Re: Why AW01N show negative Net Book Value
entry.content:
hi Sir,

it is happening in PRD system. if i do write up to correct the asset values, How should i clean up planned depreciation?

Thanks,

RTN

entry.title: SAP ECC - Sales Bill of Material
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

I’m currently facing a pricing challenge and would appreciate your guidance.

Scenario:

I have Products A, B, C, and D. Each product’s standard price is maintained via VK11 — for example, each is priced at $20.

  • When I create a sales order for any individual product, the price reflects correctly, e.g., Product A = $20.

  • I have created a Sales Bill of Material (BOM) where Product A is the Parent, and Products B, C, and D are the Child components.

  • When I create a sales order using the BOM with Product A as the Parent, the pricing automatically populates for each item based on VK11, so I see:

    • Product A = $20

    • Product B = $20

    • Product C = $20

    • Product D = $20

Additionally, other condition types apply (e.g., standard discounts).

What I want to achieve:

When creating a sales order with the BOM using Product A as the Parent, I want the child products (B, C, and D) to have a fixed price of $1 each instead of $20.

My questions:

  1. Do I need to create a new Condition Type to override the standard prices derived from VK11/VK13 for the child components? But I want to maintain individual price for product B,C and D in VK13, in case I want to raise separate Sales Order for product B,C and D without BOM.

  2. If yes, could you please provide an example of how to configure such a Condition Type, including any key settings or pointers?

entry.content: Please share the screen shot from co09 for the same combination of plant and material.  If unrestricted stock is available, it should show in the first line in co09.

entry.title: SAP Fiori for SAP S/4HANA – Which notification to use when – Notifications for Admins and Developers
entry.content:
TL;DR – Understand the basics behind notifications such as the Notification Channel and the Notification Providers including: what's involved in creating a custom Notification Provider for your own use cases, FAQ, and troubleshooting tips.  These notifications are relevant for Flexible Workflow, classic Workflow, Situation Handling (standard and extended), Smart Business KPI alerts, and process-specific notifications. This blog post is provided for Administrators and Developers. 

A very common business requirement is to notify someone that something has happened and, if possible, get them started on responding to it. 

In this blog post - Part 4 - as an administrator or developer you will learn what's "behind the covers" of notifications. This includes:

  • Configuring the Notification Channel and Notification Hub - how notifications are collected and distributed to users
  • Enabling Notifications in the SAP Fiori launchpad - settings you can adjust if needed
  • Authorizing Users for Notifications - making sure users can trigger and receive notifications
  • Activating Notification Providers - that generate the notifications for different use cases
  • Understanding the Notification Provider class - if you need to dig deeper to confirm provided notification features
  • Creating your own custom Notification Provider - where to find example code
  • Federating notifications to SAP Build Work Zone - including Secure Store and Forward (SSF) and sensitive data options
  • Providing push notifications
  • Echoing notifications to email 
  • FAQ and troubleshooting tips - including how to find notifications at runtime, as in the example below using ABAP Development Tools (ADT in Eclipse)

Finding notification runtime details in ABAP Development Tools in Eclipse

In other blog post of this series you will learn:

  • Part 1 - SAP Fiori notification techniques available in SAP S/4HANA and when to use them; when to use email notifications to complement or supplement SAP Fiori notifications. SAP Fiori for SAP S/4HANA – Which notification to use when – Workflow, Situation Handling, KPIs, etc
  • Part 2 - The impact on business users in SAP Fiori for SAP S/4HANA – Which notification to use when – Impact on business users   
  • Part 3 - For Key Users, Functional Consultants and UX Architects: Where & how to configure each notification technique in SAP Fiori for SAP S/4HANA – Which notification to use when – Where and how to configure

Configuring the Notification Channel and Notification Hub

Behind every notification is a Notification Provider that generates the notifications and provides the notification features. 

Worth knowing: More than 80 notification providers are delivered with SAP S/4HANA.

The Notification Channel collects the notifications from the active Notification Providers into the Notification Channel Hub – from where they are distributed to users.

The Notification Channel is a capability of the ABAP Platform's SAP Gateway Foundation, that uses ABAP Push Channels.  ABAP Push Channels (APC) enable bi-directional communication between the ABAP Platform and Users - even when the user has not initiated a request.  For the notification channel, ABAP Push Channels use the integration with WebSockets.  Refer to

  • SAP Help – ABAP Platform – Gateway Foundation – Notification Channel
  • SAP Help - ABAP Platform - ABAP Development Tools: User Guide - Working with ABAP Push Channels

There is a small amount of once-off configuration that you - as an administrator - complete to activate the Notification Channel and Notification Hub.  This configuration can be found in the Implementation Guide (transaction SPRO) in section ABAP Platform > Notification Channel. Refer to

  • SAP Help – ABAP Platform – Gateway Foundation – Notification Channel Configuration. 

You will need authorizations to complete this configuration - templates are provided for Hub and Channel administrators.  Refer to 

  • SAP Help – ABAP Platform – Gateway Foundation - Notification Channel User Roles and Authorizations

 

The configuration includes mapping and activating the push channel for notifications SAP_WEBSOCKET  in transaction /IWNGW/VNPUSHP.

 

Enabling Notifications in the SAP Fiori launchpad

Notifications are a standard cross-functional feature of the SAP Fiori launchpad and are typically activated by default as part of running the task list SAP_FIORI_FOUNDATION_S4 and selecting the task Configure FLP for Notification (/UI2/FLP_CUS_CONF). 

Hint: For many SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition customers, your ECS team will already have run these for you. You can review which task lists have been run previously in transaction STC02.

If you need to review or adjust the standard settings refer to: SAP Help - SAP Fiori launchpad - Administration guide - Enabling Notifications in the Launchpad

Authorizing Users for Notifications

Before using notifications, your security administrator must authorize users to trigger (produce) and receive (consume) notifications. This is a standard ABAP authorization and you can use the provided Insert Templates options to simplify adding these authorizations to your roles - templates /IWNGW/RT_USER_PRODU and /IWNGW/RT_USER_CONSU respectively.  For example, you can use the templates in Role Maintenance transaction PFCG in the Change Authorizations dialog. Refer to

  • SAP Help – ABAP Platform – Gateway Foundation - Notification Channel User Roles and Authorizations
  • SAP Note 2954378 - The Gateway Error Log shows: No authorization to access service group '/IWNGW/NOTIFICATION'

Important: Because you usually want all users to potentially trigger or receive notifications, you should consider adding these authorizations to a role assigned to every user of the SAP Fiori launchpad (FLP). For example, you might add them to a custom version of the provided every FLP user role Z_FIORI_FOUNDATION_USER.

  • This role is generated when you run task list SAP_FIORI_FOUNDATION_S4 when you set up your SAP Fiori launchpad for SAP S/4HANA system initially.
  • The role generation task can be re-run when you upgrade your SAP S/4HANA system to add new features.  So when using a custom version of the role, make sure you cross-check the generated role for authorizations change you may need to include in your custom every FLP user role.

Testing the Notification Channel

You can run the transaction /IWNGW/BEP_DEMO to test that the notification channel hub is working.  You will need to activate the demo notification provider - i.e. tick a checkbox - before running the program. Refer to SAP Help – ABAP Platform – Gateway Foundation – Testing the Demo Provider

You can choose from 2 types of demo notification – Leave Request or Purchase Order – and which user will receive the notifications. Run the program (select Execute) to generate the selected demo notifications to the selected user.

The program will confirm if a notification has been created and provide the technical runtime id.

The demo notifications will appear in the launchpad of the selected user as per the below screenshot.

 

Understanding notification providers

Notification Providers generate the notifications at runtime. 

Each Notification Provider in SAP S/4HANA is technically an ABAP Class that supports the ABAP Interface /IWNGW/IF_NOTIF_PROVIDER or /IWNGW/IF_NOTIF_PROVIDER_EXT.

Worth knowing: The interface /IWNGW/IF_NOTIF_PROVIDER_EXT provides additional options for notification types, for example where you want to support echoing of notifications to email.

 

 

You can view all the notification providers and their underlying ABAP Classes in transaction /IWNGW/BEP_NPREG.   

To use the notification types, the notification provider must be marked as active in transaction /IWNGW/VB_REG_P.  This is typically performed by an Administrator, alongside the other prerequisites for the notification channel.

Hint: As a minimum you are recommended to activate at least the most commonly used cross-functional notification providers, i.e. 

  • Workflow - provider SAP_BUSINESS_WORKFLOW
  • Situation Handling - providers SIT_NOTIF_PROV, SIT_NOTIF_PROV_EXT, SIT2_NOTIF_PROV
  • Smart Business KPI - provider /SSB/NOTIFICATION_PROVIDER
  • Demo (for verifying the notification channel is working) - provider /IWNGW/DEMO_EXT

 

A notification provider can support multiple notification types.

Each notification type is assigned a unique Notification Type key.

For each Notification Type, the provider manages:

  • Notification Type name shown in the SAP Fiori launchpad – User Actions - Settings – Notifications section where the user sets their notification preferences
  • Subject text of the notification type
  • Long text of the notification type
  • Optionally, parameters that can be included in the text(s)
  • Optionally, quick actions for the notification type
  • Optionally, whether notifications of this notification type can be grouped (type “Is Groupable”)
  • Optionally, whether notifications of this notification type can be echoed to email (type “Is Email Enabled”)
  • Optionally, version id for the notification type

Creating Custom Notification Providers

YES! You can create custom notification providers creating your own notification provider class.

You will need an ABAP Developer to create the notification provider class. 

Your ABAP Developer will need authorizations to create the class as provided by role template /IWNGW/RT_NP_DEV. Refer to:

  • SAP Help – ABAP Platform – Gateway Foundation - Notification Channel User Roles and Authorizations

You will find example code for the class in SAP Help – ABAP Platform – Gateway Foundation – Develop Notification Provider and the example code in Sample Provider.  This includes a Call Notification API you can use to test your provider.

Once your custom notification provider class is available, to generate notifications you will first need to register your custom notification provider class in transaction /IWNGW/BEP_NPREG, and activate your custom notification provider in transaction /IWNGW/VB_REG_P so that it is included in the notification push channel.

Federating notifications to SAP Build Work Zone

If you are using SAP S/4HANA alongside other SAP Cloud solutions, you should consider providing a unified experience across your solutions by using the SAP Fiori launchpad in SAP Build Work Zone, standard edition. 

Important: Make sure your notifications are working in your local SAP Fiori launchpad of SAP S/4HANA before federating them to SAP Build Work Zone. 

You need to set up some configuration in SAP Build Work Zone and SAP S/4HANA. This includes activating the push channel for SAP_CLOUD - among other prerequisites as shown below. 

Important: Do not leave this configuration in an inconsistent state - as it can cause errors impacting running processes such as flexible workflow and situations. 

Refer to:

  • SAP Help - SAP Build Work Zone, standard edition – Administration – Managing Notifications
  • SAP Help - SAP Gateway - Notification Channel - Add Push for SAP Business Technology Platform

When sending notifications to the internet you should consider whether you want to apply Secure Store and Forward (SSF) & other additional protections for potentially sensitive data.  This requires:

  • SSF to be configured in SAP Build Work Zone
  • Sensitive Data Cache to be set in the ABAP Platform (SAP Gateway) of your SAP S/4HANA system
  • Notification Providers to provide sensitive texts (for internet notifications) versus non-sensitive texts (when in SAP S/4HANA directly) - see the examples in blog post Part 3 

Refer to

  • SAP Help - SAP Build Work Zone, standard edition – Administration - Enabling Notifications From SAP S/4HANA
  • SAP Help - SAP Gateway  - Notification Channel - Activate Sensitive Data Cache and Data Encryption
  • SAP Help - SAP Gateway - Notification Channel - Notification Channel Secure Data Store

Providing push notifications

If your users frequently use mobile devices for their work tasks, you may want to consider push notifications. 

Hint: Mobile devices includes tablets, smartphones, foldable phones, smartwatches, Apple Vision Pro, etc.

The recommended approach is to use the launchpad on SAP Mobile Start to provide push notifications on mobile devices. Both Apple and Android devices are supported. To use SAP Mobile Start you must first configure SAP Build Work Zone, standard edition.

Refer to:

  • SAP Help - SAP Mobile Start - Administration guide - Configuration - Setting up SAP Mobile Start - Notifications
  • SAP Help - SAP Build Work Zone, standard edition - Administration - SAP Mobile Start - Setting Up the SAP Mobile Start Application

Hint: The earlier use of SAP Mobile Platform (SMP) for push notifications is no longer recommended as SMP has been sunset. Refer to SAP Note 3338016 - What is the future of SAP Mobile Platform on-premise beyond 2023?

Echoing notifications to email

Some users may prefer to receive SAP Fiori notifications via email.  This is particularly common where there are a large amount of casual/infrequent users. 

There are 3 configurations to check, followed by advice on how to confirm which notifications are email-enabled at runtime.

  1. Per system, th ABAP Push Channel assigned to SAP_EMAIL for email notifications
  2. Per notification type, the provider must have enabled email notifications
  3. Per user, the user must have enabled echoing their notifications of the relevant notification type to emails

Per system – verify the ABAP Push Channel for email is activated 

Push channels are configured in transaction /IWNGW/VNPUSHP. For Email Notifications, enter the push channel id SAP_EMAIL and mark it as Is Active - as shown in the example below. 

Once the push channel is active, users will see the Email option in the SAP Fiori launchpad > User Actions > Settings > Notifications for relevant notifications.

Worth knowing: If you are using the SAP Fiori launchpad of SAP Build Work Zone, you can also provide echo of  notifications to email. Refer to SAP Help - SAP Build Work Zone - Admin - Managing Notifications - Configuring an SMTP Mail Destination

Per notification type – the notification provider must permit echo to email notification

  1. Choose the Notification Type for which you want to enable email Notification.
  2. In the Notification Provider class, set the attribute IS_EMAIL_ENBLD to X

Per User – the user opts to echo notifications to email 

Prerequisite: As a user, you must have your email address maintained in the User Master maintained by your administrator - i.e. in transaction SU01. As a business user you can confirm your email address in the SAP Fiori launchpad > User Actions > Settings > User Account as shown in the examples below.

 

To set your personal notification preferences, you must:

  1. Login to SAP Fiori launchpad
  2. Navigate to User Actions - Settings - Notifications.
  3. Enabled echo to email for each notification type by ticking the Email checkbox and then selecting Save.

Any notifications triggered after you the change - that are enabled for email - will send the email notification to the user’s email address.

Further information on how to configure Email Notifications can be found in this blog post Enable Email Notification in Notification Channel Framework

FAQ and troubleshooting tips

Questions covered:

  • As an administrator, how can I see which notification texts are generated for users?
  • As an administrator, how can I check which notifications are email enabled at runtime?
  • As an administrator, can I clear/delete notifications on behalf of a user?

 

As an administrator, how can I see which notification texts are generated for users?

For workflow notifications, you can review the assigned texts in transaction SWF_PUSH_NOTIF1

For situation notifications, you can review the assigned texts in SAP Fiori apps Manage Situation Types and Manage Situation Types – Extended.

For process-specific workflows, the easiest option is to trigger the notification and use ADT in Eclipse (or transaction SE16) to check the notification cache table /IWNGW/NTYPE_TT for the template text generated.

To confirm the source notification provider, you can map the value of Notification Type Id to the entry in table /IWNGW/NTYPE.

Hint: This is one of very few areas in SAP S/4HANA were there is no CDS View provided.

As an administrator, how can I check which notifications are email enabled at runtime?

As an administrator, you can use some simple checks to review the underlying runtime data to check what is happening.  

In table /IWNGW/NTYPE you can see whether email notifications are enabled for each combination of notification provider + notification type + notification type version.  For example, you can view the content in ADT in Eclipse (or transaction SE16).

As an administrator, can I find out who whether notifications have been seen or read by users? 

Yes, you can find these details by evaluating the CDS Database View /IWNGW/INCONSMPT 

As an administrator, can I clear/delete notifications on behalf of a user?

Yes, by running transaction /IWNGW/H_CLEAR_NOTIF

Becoming a SAP Fiori for SAP S/4HANA guru

You’ll find much more on our  SAP Fiori for SAP S/4HANA topic page

Brought to you by the SAP S/4HANA RIG and Customer Care team.

entry.title: Re: SAP ECC - Sales Bill of Material
entry.content: Ideally, the item category should be different for normal sales and BOM components in sales order.  Not sure, how it is configured in your case.  Are you changing it manually or maintained the same item category? May be you can consider a workaround such that insert the item category field PSTYV to an access sequence and maintain the required price in vk11.  By doing so, when you change the item category manually in sales order, the price will get updated accordingly.

entry.title: Updating the CMS repository database information in Windows after a database upgrade
entry.content:
The Oracle 12c database where our SAP BusinessObjects Business Intelligence platform CMS and auditing tables are installed is being upgraded to Oracle 19c and onto a new database server.  The Oracle database administrator will complete the conversion and load the Oracle 19c database tables with the data from the Oracle 12c database, including the CMS and auditing tables.   Once the conversion is completed, the Oracle 12c database will be stopped and the Oracle 19c database will be in use.

Here are the specifics of my installation:
SAP BusinessObjects Business Intelligence platform installation is BI 4.3 SP03 Patch 900 
Database is Oracle 12c (going to Oracle 19c)
Windows 2022 server

I will need to update the CMS System Database Configuration in the Central Configuration Manager(CCM). 

I think I can follow KBA 2545374 - How to update the CMS database information in Windows but want to verify this will be the correct KBA to follow.

https://me.sap.com/notes/2545374

The resolution for KBA 2545374 states the following:

Resolution
1.Open the Central Configuration Manager (CCM)
2.Stop the Server Intelligence Agent (SIA)
3.Right click the stopped SIA and click 'properties'
4.Go to the 'Configuration' tab
5.In the CMS System Database Configuration, click 'Specify...'
6.Select "Update data source settings" and click ok
7.Select the new database driver and click ok
8.Select your data source, enter your authentication information/configuration and click ok
9.Enter your cluster key (note: this can be found in the 'Configuration' tab for the SIA) and click ok

Are there examples that show what steps 7 and 8 will look like as I follow the KBA steps?  Sometimes having a visual of what the screens will look like are very helpful.
